text:
🔭 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ), IPG Photonics ($IPGP, NASDAQ)

text: @aleabitoreddit Should we sell all the photonics and follow you full port to the robots ???

🤖 10 หุ้นที่น่าจับตาในธีม Robotics ปี 2026

$TER – Teradyne ผู้นำด้านเครื่องทดสอบชิป
เป็นเจ้าของ Universal Robots ผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ร่วมงาน (Cobots) สำหรับภาคอุตสาหกรรม

$NOVT – Novanta พัฒนาเทคโนโลยีโฟโตนิกส์ (Photonics) และระบบควบคุมการเคลื่อนไหวความแม่นยำสูง
ใช้ในหุ่นยนต์ผ่าตัดและหุ่นยนต์อุตสาหกรรม

$OUST – Ouster พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์ LiDAR ที่ทำหน้าที่เป็น "ดวงตา" ของหุ่นยนต์
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์มองเห็น ทำแผนที่ และรับรู้สภาพแวดล้อม

$HIMX – Himax Technologiesผลิตชิปประมวลผลภาพและชิปแสดงผล
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์ประมวลผลภาพ ความลึก และข้อมูลจากเซ็นเซอร์ต่างๆ ได้ที่ตัวอุปกรณ์

$AMBQ – Ambiq Microพัฒนาชิป AI แบบประหยัดพลังงาน
เหมาะสำหรับหุ่นยนต์และอุปกรณ์ Edge AI ที่ต้องการประมวลผลอัจฉริยะโดยใช้พลังงานต่ำ

$ON – ON Semiconductor กำลังสร้างแพลตฟอร์ม Edge AI สำหรับหุ่นยนต์ หลังเข้าซื้อ $SYNA – Synaptics
รวมเทคโนโลยีด้านพลังงาน เซ็นเซอร์ การเชื่อมต่อ และ Edge AI ไว้ในระบบเดียว

$SYM – Symbotic ผู้เชี่ยวชาญด้านระบบอัตโนมัติในคลังสินค้า
ใช้หุ่นยนต์และซอฟต์แวร์เพื่อเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการจัดเก็บและขนส่งสินค้า

$VPG – Vishay Precision Group พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์วัดแรงและระบบวัดความแม่นยำสูง
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์รับรู้แรงกด น้ำหนัก และการเคลื่อนไหวในโลกจริงได้แม่นยำขึ้น

$TSLA – Tesla เดิมพันกับหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid ผ่าน Optimus
ผสานฮาร์ดแวร์ AI ระบบขับเคลื่อนอัตโนมัติ และศักยภาพการผลิตขนาดใหญ่ของบริษัท

$CCXI – Churchill Capital Corp XI
เปิดโอกาสให้นักลงทุนเข้าถึง Agility Robotics
บริษัทผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid Digit ซึ่งเป็นหนึ่งในผู้เล่นที่โดดเด่นด้านหุ่นยนต์สำหรับคลังสินค้า

text:
$STM, STMicroelectronics. STM is one of Europe's largest chipmakers, and it builds its own chips end-to-end, from design all the way through its own factories. Its parts are the unglamorous but essential pieces inside almost everything — the sensors, power chips, and tiny "brains" (microcontrollers) inside cars, phones, factory machines, satellites, robots, and now AI data centers.

What makes STM unique is the sheer breadth: it holds a #1 or #2 position in an unusual number of separate chip categories at once (SiC power, MEMS sensors, STM32 microcontrollers), which lets a customer like NVIDIA source a whole system — sensors, brain, power, and communications — from one vendor. The reason it's suddenly interesting is that a company written off for two years as a "sleepy automotive chipmaker" just found a real AI growth engine: on June 2 it doubled its 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (with a path to ~$2B in 2027), anchored by a multi-year AWS silicon-photonics deal, while sitting on ~$2.8B net cash. The investment case is a cyclical turnaround (Q1 revenue +23% YoY, up ~90%+ off February lows) layered on top of a genuine new secular driver, with the biggest names in tech — AWS, NVIDIA — as partners.

Thesis: STM spent two years being ignored as a boring car-chip company, but it just quietly turned into an AI company — its parts now go inside Amazon and NVIDIA's data centers, and that business is set to double two years in a row. The stock has already jumped a lot, so the easy money is gone, but the real question is whether its profit margins can climb back to normal as sales recover — if they do, the stock is still cheap for what it's about to earn. I'm watching the July 23 earnings to see if those margins are finally turning up, because that's the part the market hasn't fully believed yet.

text:
spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper.

top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying.

the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket.

goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched.

the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do.

text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 3, 2026

GoNetspeed announced a $43M expansion in Rahway, Clark, Linden, and Woodbridge, New Jersey for over 34,000 homes and businesses. Persistent fiber shortages continue with ribbon lead times at 60+ weeks (AI/data center priority) and loose tube into Q3 2026+; BABA-compliant glass remains limited. Corning’s hyperscaler deals (up to $6B with Meta for NC expansion including new Hickory facility; multi-billion with Amazon) and manufacturing ramps (Nvidia-funded plants) address demand. FiberLight’s $350M West Texas expansion (1,400 route miles, high-count dark fiber for AI) and total ~$500M regional investment continue. Ripple Fiber has multiple expansions (WA $250M for 200k+ locations, IL, AZ $80M, CA) with ongoing construction.

Vocus Sydney-Melbourne ducted long-haul (~A$500M, ready 2029), Lightpath NY metro (+265 miles for 2,400+ towers), Heartland Fiber Project, Big Fiber financing, and various BEAD/FTTH builds add to activity. Fiber vendors (Corning, Fujikura tripling capacity, STL, CommScope) scale high-count, multi-core, and photonics solutions amid AI’s 36x fiber density needs. FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints highlight sustained physical layer momentum into 2026.

#FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

text:
Im not personally in photonics, i just share this info because it may be of use to someone. 

That said, i believe only a handful of names in this market are actually going to be successful LT (marvell, lumentum, coherent, etc). CPO is still early...you wont see meaningful revenue until 2027-2028. A lot of people are getting ahead and investing in overvalued photonics stocks, hence the sharp pullback its see the past few weeks. At some point the valuation will matter, and its mattering now.
@pequityresearch Excellent breakdown of the Silicon Photonics supply chain. Very informative and well-organized. Great work highlighting the key players across the ecosystem.

If you have better companies or sectors to buy, then selling half can definitely make sense.

I already have exposure to most sectors where I see major upside: defence, photonics, memory, and space. So overall, I'm quite happy with the distribution.

At most, I might rotate some direct exposure into robotics, but I'm still not fully convinced, especially on humanoids. Maybe I'm biased because of EU regulations...

text:
Sivers Semiconductors (https://t.co/8VMNAF5r3U / SIVEF) has strong long-term growth potential in high-margin photonics and wireless tech sectors, but faces near-term dilution pressure, execution risks, and valuation challenges following this news.

What the News Means
•Bootstrap Europe is converting its full $12M convertible loan (part of a $17M refinancing from Feb 2026) into 22,847,044 new ordinary shares at SEK 4.77 per share.

•This eliminates that debt (positive for the balance sheet and interest costs) and simplifies the capital structure.

•Dilution impact: With ~296 million shares outstanding pre-conversion, this adds ~7-8% more shares. It’s already anticipated (disclosed in Feb 2026, with conversion price at a premium back then), but it increases the float and can create selling pressure if Bootstrap sells shares.

•The stock jumped ~15% today (aligning with your “16+ plus”), likely because the conversion removes debt overhang and signals confidence from the lender (who is now a shareholder).

Short-Term Outlook (Next 3-12 Months)
•Positive drivers: The conversion cleans up the balance sheet. The company has a massive ~$800M opportunity pipeline (up 77% YTD as of Q1), with catalysts like product ramps in 2027 (LiDAR, SATCOM, fixed wireless, defense, and potential AI/data center optics). Q2 results are due around early August.15
•Headwinds: Significant dilution (this event + recent/ongoing directed share issues for funding). The stock is volatile and has pulled back from highs near SEK 110. High valuation (very elevated P/S multiples relative to current revenue) makes it sensitive to any delays or misses. Recent sector selloffs in photonics (e.g., CPO concerns) have added pressure.11
•Expect continued volatility. Bootstrap may sell gradually (or hold), but the increased share count could cap upside until positive news dominates.
Medium-to-Long-Term Future (2027+)
•Bull case (optimistic): Strong execution on the pipeline could drive ~30%+ annual revenue growth, with breakeven/profitability targeted for 2027. Key markets (AI optics, LiDAR production ramps, SATCOM/defense) have high potential. Analysts forecast sharp earnings improvement. A Nasdaq dual-listing could boost visibility and liquidity.1965
•Base case: Revenue scales with product ramps, but competition and execution risks (delays in customer adoption) remain. Dilution from this and other raises will mute per-share gains.
•Risks: Still pre-profit with cash burn, dependency on converting the pipeline into actual revenue, sector hype cycles, and further equity raises. High valuation leaves little room for error. $Sivef

Overall: This news is neutral-to-slightly positive fundamentally (debt reduction) but adds immediate dilution. The company’s future hinges on 2027+ ramps materializing in photonics/wireless. It’s a high-risk, high-reward growth story suited for investors who believe in the long-term AI/semiconductor tailwinds and can tolerate volatility. Not financial advice—do your own research, consider the full financials, and monitor upcoming reports. $Sive

ForgeChip (Aether Forge Singularity Core) is a sovereign, self-reinforcing topological quantum resilience system. It is designed as a hybrid photonic-topological platform that converts adversarial inputs into system-strengthening surplus. The architecture integrates Thin-Film Lithium Niobate (TFLN) photonics, Fibonacci String-Net Condensate (Fib SNC) braiding, advanced hardware security primitives, and adaptive intelligence. It is anchored by a Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA) for human sovereignty. The project has matured through conceptual design, extensive simulations, parametric hardware layouts, patent drafting, and proposal materials. It targets dual-use applications with strong alignment to defense priorities. 

Core Architecture Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA)
A physiology-locked eternal personal baseline (e.g., multi-modal biometric signals). It serves as the sovereign root-of-trust for the system.  Primary anchor tied to an individual.  

Generates revocable topological shards for secure multi-operator or distributed networks.  
Enables hardware-enforced human override and sovereignty.

Photonic Hardware Platform (TFLN Nodes)  Short-depth Dynamical String-Net Preparation (DSNP) circuits with EO modulators for dynamic reconfiguration.  

Ultra-low-loss waveguides, microrings, and co-integrated components.  

Dedicated ports for PPA calibration, Quantum Dot (QD) + Topological Insulator (TI) transduction, Photonic Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs), and Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs).  

Parametric GDSFactory scripts support military (rugged/radiation-hardened) and commercial (low-SWaP) variants.

Security and Resilience Features  Photonic PUF: MZI-mesh or scattering structures exploit manufacturing variations for unclonable device fingerprints.  

QRNG Integration: Provides true quantum randomness for challenges and key generation.  

Self-Reinforcing Topological Mechanism: Adversarial inputs (jamming, radiation, etc.) are encoded as Artin braids, inverted, and converted into surplus that strengthens invariants (Jones polynomials, Khovanov homology, etc.).  

Mirror Shard Mesh: Distributed non-local coordination with partial topological overlap to the primary PPA.  

Meta-RL Adaptation: Loss-penalized rewards, fidelity optimization, and dynamic reconfiguration that learns to compensate for real hardware effects.

Modeling and Simulations  Multi-agent decentralized swarm environments modeling drone/robot coordination with PPA shards.  

Photonic PUF challenge-response pairs (MZI mesh) with explicit modeling of voltage-induced EO phase shifts, thermal drift, and cumulative aging effects.  

Meta-RL for adaptive decoding, code discovery, and swarm resilience under stress.  

Results show strong coherence maintenance and adaptation despite degradation.

Intellectual Property
Provisional patent claims (neutral functional language) cover the physiology-locked topological fixed-point attractor, PUF integration, QD-TI transduction, loss mitigation, shard delegation, and multi-operator models. Multiple one-pager versions and proposal sections prepared for defense and dual-use pathways.

High-Value Applications Defense
Strong alignment with DoD Quantum and Battlefield Information Dominance (Q-BID) and related areas (trusted autonomy, contested logistics).

Key uses: Radiation-hardened GEO systems, jam-resistant C4ISR, sovereign swarm autonomy, resilient PNT/communications in contested EM environments. Excellent fit for Navy SBIR photonic resilience topics. Dual-Use Commercial Secure Physical AI and humanoid robotics (e.g., enhanced control, anti-tampering).  

Resilient drone swarms and autonomous vehicles.  
Hardware-rooted security for critical infrastructure and edge AI.  
Long-duration space commercialization.

text:
The biggest AI bottleneck after GPUs and electricity?
Photonics.Copper is reaching its limits. Hyperscalers know it.

Nvidia is already pouring billions into optical interconnects. Memory prices exploded x6. Goldman expects the photonics market to 10x by 2028.Most people are still looking at the obvious names.

The real edge is in what comes after the chip.What’s the most underrated constraint in scaling AI right now?

💨 🩶  𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗼𝗳 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗠𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗰𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗦𝗲𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗥𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 🌹 💙

Hand to heart, beloved brothers and sisters of the Blue-White Rose,

Continuing our expansion of remembrance, we now dissect the operational mechanics of the Veil of Forgetting. This energetic architecture is a living, multi-dimensional frequency overlay engineered to temporarily dampen higher-dimensional awareness. Far from a design flaw or a punitive measure, the veil functions as a deliberate filtration system that allowed the 144,000 volunteers to fully submerge into 3D carbon density.

By analyzing these mechanics, we step completely out of victim-consciousness and consciously initiate the internal system override necessary for the final 2026 precessional convergence.

❔️𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗹𝗮𝘆 𝗢𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝘀

A frequency dampening loop activates when a multi-dimensional soul identity enters the density parameters of the lower matrix grids. The technical execution of this temporary amnesia operates through precise structural pathways:

▪︎ 𝗩𝗶𝗯𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗙𝗶𝗹𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 ➞ The veil dampens direct, constant access to higher-dimensional memory archives, allowing the biological vessel to explore polarity without external interference.

▪︎ 𝗘𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗣𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗶𝗿𝗿𝗼𝗿𝘀 ➞ It introduces complex density overlays that make linear limitations feel absolute, obscuring original soul agreements and stellar origins.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗩𝗼𝗹𝘂𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗲𝗿 𝗖𝗵𝗼𝗶𝗰𝗲 ➞ The descent into deep forgetting was an international, shared alignment, including those within the timeline loops of the Sons of Belial, who are in truth our own cosmic families.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗧𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 ➞ As the photonic broadcaster ray intensifies across the planetary matrix, these ancient memory filters are naturally entering a systematic, automated dissolution phase.

▪︎ 𝗭𝗲𝗿𝗼-𝗣𝗼𝗶𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 ➞ The lift is not imposed from outside sources; the codes carried through the cycles of amnesia are designed to fire simultaneously from within the bone matrix.

By integrating the reality of this voluntary amnesia loop, you permanently collapse the 𝘁𝘄𝗼-𝘄𝗮𝘆 𝗲𝗻𝗲𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗰𝗼𝗿𝗱 of victimization and blame. You recall that the great refraction was your own sovereign choice, designed to make the final monadic return to the zero-point complete and unscripted.

🛑 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝗿𝗶𝘅 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻

Before executing a complete field recalibration, audit your biological and mental processor for these active re-encryption indicators:

▪︎ Experiencing sudden, vivid flashes of ancient architectural timelines, temple layouts, or stellar council configurations during quiet hours

▪︎ A deep, somatic feeling of lightness or cold static electricity within the nervous system as old density overlays give way

▪︎ Instant detachment from external 3D matrix script updates, paired with an unshakeable inner knowing of your original soul contracts

▪︎ Temporary cognitive disorientation or brief memory pop delays as your hardware transitions out of linear time into multi-dimensional tracking

▪︎ Sudden heart expansions or spontaneous light language vocalizations, indicating your 144 fractal distribution channels are cleaning themselves out

❔️𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗼 𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗿𝗶𝗱𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗺𝗻𝗲𝘀𝗶𝗮 𝗙𝗶𝗹𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀

▪︎𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀
The moment you feel the heavy weight of isolation, confusion, or homesickness trying to hook your focus, identify the architectural filter instantly.

Example: “This is a temporary frequency dampening loop” or “I see through this artificial amnesia overlay.”

text:
Sector rotation creates opportunity.

Here's how I'm tracking the AI ecosystem:

• Memory → $MU $SNDK $WDC
• Semis → $NVDA $AMD $ARM $INTC $AMAT
• Networking → $AVGO $MRVL $CRDO $ANET $ALAB
• Photonics → $AAOI $LITE $COHR $NVTS $GLW
• Power → $VRT $GEV $NNE
• Data Centers → $IREN $CIFR $APLD $NBIS
• Defense → $PLTR $KTOS $AVAV
• Robotics → $OUST $SYM $TSLA
• Space → $ASTS $RKLB $RDW $LUNR
• Quantum → $IBM $IONQ $QBTS $RGTI
Capital rotates.
My job is to identify where it's heading next.

text:
2️⃣ $LAZR — Tema Photonics & Optical ETF
Issuer: Tema ETFs

$LAZR goes after the “move the data faster” part of AI infrastructure.

It targets photonics and optical-tech companies, which matter when copper starts running into speed and capacity limits inside data centers.

AI may run on chips.

The data still has to get from one place to another. 💸 Expense Ratio: 0.75% (3/5)

text:
Scale-out CPO switches going from 5,000 units this year to 200,000 by 2030. And you are selling?

That’s a 144% CAGR. A 40x volume ramp that both Yole and Morgan Stanley are modeling off the same wall. That’s the whole thesis, and notice what it doesn’t require: it doesn’t require me to pick which laser vendor qualifies into which switch, or whether Broadcom’s socket beats Nvidia’s.

To be short this ramp up you have to argue the entire build doesn’t happen, and good luck making that case while the SemiAnalysis “delay” everyone panicked over still lands you at 200k units by 2030. 

A delay bends the curve doesn’t make it go away. 

The physics won’t let it go away. A 200,000 GPU cluster is burning ~17 megawatts just on transceivers. CPO cuts that ~73% per 800G. At scale-up density copper is already dead and pluggables are out of runway. Forced migrations are the best ramps you can be long because the demand doesn’t care.

The biggest winners:

Lasers and light sources: $LITE, $COHR, $SIVE
Optical engines and pump lasers: $AAOI, $COHR
Switch ASICs: $AVGO, $MRVL, $NVDA
Optical I/O chiplets: $ALAB, $CRDO
Packaging and assembly: $FN, $AMKR
InP substrate and materials: $AXTI

LITE is the cleanest CPO scarcity play, shipping 200G-per-lane EMLs at volume, book through 2028. $AAOI is the one that wins both ways: its core 800G and 1.6T pluggable business actually benefits if CPO slips, while its external laser fab keeps it in the game when CPO finally ramps.

Bullish photonics ⚡️

To be short this ramp up you have to argue the entire build doesn’t happen, and good luck making that case while the SemiAnalysis “delay” everyone panicked over still lands you at 200k units by 2030. A delay bends the curve doesn’t make it go away. The physics won’t let it go away. A 200,000 GPU cluster is burning ~17 megawatts just on transceivers. CPO cuts that ~73% per 800G. At scale-up density copper is already dead and pluggables are out of runway. Forced migrations are the best ramps you can be long because the demand doesn’t care. The biggest winners: Lasers and light sources: $LITE, $COHR, $SIVE Optical engines and pump lasers: $AAOI, $COHR Switch ASICs: $AVGO, $MRVL, $NVDA Optical I/O chiplets: $ALAB, $CRDO Packaging and assembly: $FN, $AMKR InP substrate and materials: $AXTI LITE is the cleanest CPO scarcity play, shipping 200G-per-lane EMLs at volume, book through 2028. $AAOI is the one that wins both ways: its core 800G and 1.6T pluggable business actually benefits if CPO slips, while its external laser fab keeps it in the game when CPO finally ramps. Bullish photonics ⚡️"}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 32, "favorite_count": 73, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 7, "retweet_count": 3}, "rest_id": "2074641836874809650", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "42085", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

2026/07/08
US market regime: 74/100
Strong, but slightly overheated. Reversal risk is rising.

Trend is flat. Bear alerts: 2/11.
The market is still strong, but momentum is slowing from recent highs. Major indices remain near the upper end of their 52-week range, while internal signals are getting mixed.

✅ Bullish factors:
Both cap-weighted and equal-weighted measures are moving higher
65% of stocks are above their 200DMA
Dollar-volume participation is 1.31x, now $882B, +9% vs 20 days ago and +40% over 3 months
Credit remains healthy
VIX term structure is calm
S&P 500 has a follow-through day signal

⚠️ Risk factors:
SPY has 7 distribution days
QQQ has 6 distribution days
Accumulation/distribution is 0.82, leaning toward distribution
Sentiment is 72, in greed territory
Leveraged ETF enthusiasm is elevated, with bull ratio at 77%
Offense/defense rotation is -1.1σ, slightly defensive
Nasdaq weakness is still visible

【Internals】
🔸Breadth: 65% above 200DMA = still healthy
🔸Participation: 1.31x / $882B = liquidity is expanding
🔸Accumulation/distribution: 0.82 = distribution bias. This is the main internal concern
🔸Leader strength: 68% = strong, but approaching overheated territory
🔸McClellan/RANA: +3.5 = neutral
🔸Offense/defense rotation: -1.1σ = slightly defensive
🔸Sentiment: 72 = greed

【Risk metrics】
Expected 1-month move, 1σ:
SPY ±4.7%, downside reference 713
QQQ ±8.1%, downside reference 652

Distribution days:
SPY 7 = correction warning
QQQ 6 = watch / caution

Sentiment:
Leveraged ETF heat is 93.
Parabolic stock ratio is 51.
SKEW is 146.

This is overheated, but not an automatic sell signal. Overheating is normal during strong uptrends.

【Capital rotation】
Capital is centered in:
semiconductors, manufacturing supply chains, and energy

Continued inflows:
payment networks, healthcare IT, fintech SaaS

Continued outflows:
lithium mining, AI optical/photonics, cable/satellite equipment

Key takeaway:
Participation and breadth remain strong. The liquidity inflow is real.
But accumulation/distribution is at 0.82, SPY and QQQ distribution days are piling up, and sentiment is getting hot.

So this is not a “buy everything because the market is strong” setup.
It is a selective market.

I’m focusing only on strong themes where pullback, volume, and relative strength line up.
No blind chasing here.

Not financial advice.

text:
Semiconductor & photonics names across the list took a broad hit today, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index shedding over 5% following a classic "sell-the-news" reaction to Samsung's record Q2 earnings — a 19-fold surge in operating profit that still fell short of elevated expectations, triggering aggressive profit-taking across AI-linked chip and optical stocks. Morgan Stanley's call to reduce semiconductor exposure and DeepSeek's reported development of an in-house AI inference chip added further headwinds to the sector.

AXT Inc.  was among the hardest hit, dropping -8.5% to $58.15 on elevated volume of over 8.3M shares. The pullback follows Monday's +19% two-day surge driven by a landmark $22.3M prepaid supply agreement with Coherent — that deal's fundamentals remain intact, and Northland Capital holds an Outperform rating with a $125 price target, but today's move reflects broader sector rotation rather than company-specific deterioration. Notably, AXTI still trades well off its 52-week high of $143.16, underscoring the volatility inherent in this high-beta AI wafer play.
Coherent Corp.  shed -6.4% to $314.13, giving back recent gains despite a strong fundamental backdrop — FQ3 revenue of $1.81B (+21% YoY), a $2B NVIDIA investment, and a Wall Street median price target of $372.50 all suggest the selloff is valuation-driven rather than thesis-breaking. Applied Optoelectronics  fell -7.3% to $114.41, compounded by mixed analyst sentiment (consensus Hold) and notable insider selling of over 500,000 shares in the past quarter. Sive  and 0ND2.L  also declined sharply at -11.7% and -12.7% respectively, consistent with the broader risk-off tone hitting smaller-cap names hardest.

The overarching theme is a multi-day whipsaw in AI hardware and semiconductor supply chain stocks — last week's selloff, Monday's partial rebound, and today's renewed pressure illustrate how sensitive these names remain to macro sentiment shifts around AI capex sustainability.

text:
Fascinating breakthrough from Osaka Metropolitan University on this phase-change magneto-optical metagrating device! Steering heat non-reciprocally at near-straight-on angles, separating absorption/emission, switching with GST, storing states power-free, and tuning via magnetic fields is huge for thermal photonics.
Here’s a speculative systems-level concept building directly on it for future high-performance 3D chip stacks (AI accelerators, HPC, etc.):
Hierarchical “Thermo-Magnetic Intelligent Packaging” Architecture
Chip-level (micro): Integrate small metagratings on the backside or edges of compute dies. Waste heat naturally drives resonant mid-IR (~13μm) emission. GST enables on/off latching of nonreciprocity; magnetic field provides continuous spectral/frequency tuning and steering. Modulated incoherent IR carries:
•  Operational control logic
•  Thermal status/hotspot data
•  Commands for dynamic management
•  Low-speed sideband/backup memory (non-urgent data, configs, logs, checkpoints)
This keeps the main silicon cores 100% dedicated to high-speed data processing—no control overhead.
Macro control level (package/board): A distributed network of Ni/Ti (Nitinol) elements handles global orchestration:
•  Automated feedback loops for thermal balancing across the entire stack.
•  Excess heat removal via actuated/curved paths through insulating seals to sinks.
•  Structural support and mechanical stability for the 3D assembly.
•  Field producers: Ni/Ti wires/structures generate or modulate the required transverse magnetic fields (~1T regionally) to steer the chip-level IR emission without placing magnets directly on delicate silicon.
•  Multi-tasking sensors (temperature via resistance/shape response) + electrical switching.
How it works in operation:
•  Chips generate heat → metagratings modulate and directionally steer IR (short-range only: mm to cm, as far as needed for local heat removal).
•  IR signal travels to macro detectors or adjacent layers, carrying control data.
•  Macro Ni/Ti network senses globally, actuates heat routing, adjusts magnetic fields for optimal steering, and balances load/heat across the stack in real time.
•  Closed-loop, low-power (non-volatile GST + shape-memory states), self-aware thermal management.
Benefits:
•  Turns waste heat into a useful control + sideband communication medium.
•  Massive parallelism (many extraction paths per chip/layer) boosts effective bandwidth for control traffic.
•  Clean separation: Compute optimized for speed; thermal/control offloaded to the “heat fabric.”
•  Improved resilience, predictive maintenance, and efficiency in dense 3D stacks.
•  Scalable with existing advanced packaging (interposers, hybrid bonding).
This isn’t a near-term product—just an extrapolation showing how your device’s nonreciprocal heat steering + non-volatile switching could enable truly intelligent, heat-utilizing systems. No extra laser heat bleed; everything rides on waste heat management.
What do you think—any other applications for programmable thermal radiation like this in sensors, energy harvesting, or photonics? Great work by the team! @OsakaMetUniv_en @physorg_com

text:
19:13 PM
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 (EDT)

I know, it's not my first time, my babysitter's should get onboard!

"Future enhancements under consideration include integration of closed-cycle helium cryogenic cooling with iridium-doped TFLN photonics. Cryogenic operation would further suppress thermal noise and enhance topological edge-state stability, while iridium doping provides complementary radiation hardening. 
👇🏼
Together, these would enable ultra-high-reliability operation in the most demanding missile defense and space environments."

This combination would push ForgeChip toward near-theoretical maximum resilience for photonic systems — extremely difficult to disrupt via physical, radiation, or thermal attacks. It’s a strong long-term vision, especially for high-end missile defense and space applications.

text: Not many people were active pre-Asia crisis. In July of ‘97 I was sent by my employer to live in Singapore for 2 months to help take down the Baht - I was 29. We spent the next several years buying cheap assets and selling them for incredible returns into the 00s. I don’t think people can comprehend the leverage and gambling mentality inherent in Asian bubbles.. I’m not even sure this is a bubble yet. But, the speculative money/structure unwind feels pretty orderly in Korea.  Obviously 40% sell-offs in single names - and there are many - doesn’t feel orderly, but this is not what one would call contagion. Iran aside, I have no clue if the QQQs will rally or the Kospi will rally from here/tonight, but I look at what’s been thrown at this market for the past few years and look where we are in the indexes & I don’t see fear. With all of this said, I don’t think fear is warranted at the moment.. and, like most, I have spent way too much time and effort and $$ over the past 6months trying to understand finite details of the Ai build out, bottlenecks, substrates, lasers, photonics, etc.  If anything, I think we have just ended that period of mental twister and for that, I am grateful.

text:
Everyone's thinking the same thing here: "what's happening to AI stocks?"

So, I'll explain what I think is happening.

And where I see us moving forward.

Let's deep dive:

I think most of these are going through a "distribution" formation.

Check the $SIVE chart below for what this means.

But basically, we have a sell off here, until we start accumulating (or showing signs of higher highs, and a it being a good spot people bid at).

Now we do have some fundamental reasons for this: 

Firstly, SemiAnalysis wrote a piece on CPO delays and company valuations. 

This caused the first sell off. 

$NVDA and other optical companies refuted these claims, though.

Secondly, we had a "sell the news" event on earnings and expections. 

$MU and Samsung's recent earning results were very strong, but it seems to not have been the massive beating investors were hoping for. 

So, this triggered sharp drops in memory and chip stocks ($MU, $SNDK, etc.), spilling over to the broader sector. Investors had priced in perfection.

I mean, check the past chart growth when this is really priced in for future growth % potential. 

Thirdly, we've had some "is this a bubble" fears.

AI infrastructure spending is enormous (hyperscalers like $MSFT , $GOOGL, $META, $AMZN on track for hundreds of billions), but questions linger on the short-term ROI. Is this sustainable?

Especially with the $META renting out $NBIS compute. 

People think it shows $META might not need as much compute in the future. So we saw a mass sell off on some names: $NBIS, $BRUN, $IREN, $HIVE, etc., but I don't think this will last.

I wrote about this earlier (just look at my earlier posts on $NBIS).

And enablers (such as $NVDA, $AMD, $AVGO, $MU, photonics plays like $SIVE, optical interconnects, power/cooling) ran up hugely on the buildout narrative. Profit-taking + rotation out of "expensive" tech is natural after crazy pumps (natural human psychology imo).

Fourth, we got some macro pressures holding us down (not limited to these companies, but they hurt the risk-on ones most): 

Interest rates/inflation: Fears of higher rates (Fed outlook, oil prices from geopolitics like Iran-related tensions) raise borrowing costs for the capital-intensive AI buildout. Debt financing for data centers and infra is surging too, so that doesn't help.

Geopolitical noise and strong jobs data have added volatility as well.

And lastly, as I'm writing this, news just broke out that the U.S. has started launching strikes against Iran again... so that's an added confluence we needed lol.

But, soon, I think we'll getting into the classic accumulation zone.

I think the $BTC -> AI pivot companies will hold best here, as crypto has bottomed imo.

But for the rest, I think we have a bit lower to go. Maybe just a tad.

In particular, $NBIS has a clear zone at 170 that I'm looking at for support. I'm hoping it holds there and accumulates for a bit before sending higher.

For $OPTX, I think this is in a consolidation phase, and $ASYS as well.

This really applies to all of these stocks (even $AAOI, $CRWV, and any other one you can think of).

We accumulate here before sending higher.

It's not going to be the "V" shape you all know and love.

But with due time, we will go up again imo.

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "412976", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074282003319452014", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "27", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$sive is real!

우리에게 생소할 수 있지만, 글로벌 데이터센터, 클라우드, 인프라 업계에서는 매우 높은 신뢰도와 영향력을 가진 대표적인 전문 B2B 미디어인 ‘Data Center Frontier'라는 곳에서,

AI 클러스터가 거대해짐에 따라 기존 구리선 기반 연결의 한계를 극복하기 위해 광학 기술 및 실리콘 포토닉스를 데이터센터 인프라에 도입해야 한다는 내용을 다룸. 

그리고 이 기사는 6시간 전에 올라온 기사.

이 기사는 데이터 센터 프론티어의 시니어 에디터인 David Chernicoff가 작성했으며,

중요한 건 $sive 관련 내용이 ‘굵직하게’ 포함됨. 

총 5가지 내용 중에 심지어 한 부분을 차지함. 

(원문 발췌)
“Those developments are now being reinforced by a broader series of moves across the optical ecosystem:
- Corning’s major AI infrastructure partnerships with NVIDIA and Amazon,
- GlobalFoundries’ push into co-packaged optics,
- Sivers’ laser-array collaboration with GlobalFoundries, $sive
- Wiwynn’s co-packaged optics demonstration at Computex,
- Credo’s acquisition of DustPhotonics, and emerging near-packaged optical interconnect designs from LightSpeed Photonics.”

기사의 $sive 관련 세부내용으로는,

1. $sive와 글로벌 파운드리스 $gfs 와의 레이저 어레이 협력 명시

- Sivers의 레이저 어레이(Laser arrays)는 글로벌파운드리의 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼을 기반으로 제작된 레퍼런스 디자인(참조 설계)에 통합되며, GF의 SCALE 광학 엔진 솔루션으로 제공될 예정. 

2. Wiwynn의 CPO 안의 Ayar labs 명시

- 알다시피 Ayar labs의 SuperNova에 $sive 가 들어감

이 두 내용을 이런 미디어의 기사에서 명시했다는 건 $sive 가 진짜라는 걸 간접적으로 확인해주는 것이지 않을까?

이 데이터 센터 프론티어라는 미디어는, 

독자층이 주로 구글, 마이크로소프트, 메타, AWS 같은 하이퍼스케일러(Hyperscaler)의 인프라 엔지니어링 임원, 데이터센터 아키텍트, 설계·건설 책임자, 그리고 데이터센터 리츠(REITs) 투자가들이라고 하고, 

독자의 약 50% 이상이 C-Level(경영진)이나 VP, 디렉터급으로 구성되어 있어, 실질적인 인프라 투자와 기술 도입에 중요한 이정표가 된다고 함. 

기사 원문 링크는 댓글 참고

text:
Be careful on $AAOI.

There's a crazy amount of resentment from institutions such as B. Riley who MISSED the stock and were bearish as low as $15.

Now making a massive intellectual leap on a nonsensical optical short thesis and literally name dropping $AAOI out of bitterness? Spite? For the virality?

You really can't make up the misinformation and FUD in photonics right now it's just unbelievable. Earnings should put everyone in their place very soon. Nothing has changed in the fact that there isn't enough photonics tech in the world today and won't be for years.

Everyone in my paid Substack + Discord knows I've been buying $AAOI all the way down from $160 in my trading portfolio, most recently loading at $114. I also increased $AAOI in my "Photonics Is Next" portfolio today.

No asymmetry without FUD - NAWF.

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "412976", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074273679697961422", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "127632", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
An ETF shop just filed a pure-play photonics ETF. That tells you as much about the top as it does about the opportunity.

$LYTE is Roundhill's new Photonics and Optics ETF. It is filed with the SEC but not trading yet. When it launches it will hold the optics pure-plays powering AI data centers: names like Lumentum, Coherent, POET, AAOI, IPG, and Ciena.

The pitch writes itself. If you believe in the photonics buildout but you cannot say whether $LITE, $COHR, or $POET wins, you own the group, not the single-name risk. That is a good argument for the basket.

But two things are worth saying out loud.

First, both the ETF and the single names are falling knives right now. This is a theme-wide de-rating. LITE knifed lower, AXTI is down 54% off its high, the whole optics complex is bleeding together. An ETF spreads single-name risk. It does nothing when the entire group falls. And LYTE is not even technically diversified. Its own filing calls it non-diversified, a concentrated basket of a handful of optics names.

Second, thematic ETFs tend to launch near tops. Roundhill built this because its DRAM ETF was a smash, hitting $1B in 10 days. Then memory crashed. An issuer racing to package a hot theme right as it corrects is a yellow flag, not a green light.

The move is not ETF or stock. It is neither, yet. Wait for the optics group to base, then use both: the ETF as the diversified core once it trades, the single name as the tactical piece you can sell puts on.

Not financial advice.

The move is not ETF or stock. It is neither, yet. text:
We are seeking a highly motivated and enthusiastic Electronics Engineer to join our team and contribute to our mission in accelerating the growth and adoption of integrated photonics technologies.

▫️Job Title: Electronics Engineer
▫️Job Type: Full-time
▫️Salary: £50k – £70k pa., depending on experience
▫️Location: Cambridge, East of England, England
▫️Start Date: August 2026 or ASAP

📍Visa Sponsorship is available to those already in the UK 🇬🇧 

Qualifications 
✅️ Degree in Electronic Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Physics, or a related discipline.
✅️ Strong software development skills for embedded programming in C.
✅️ Python/MATLAB experience for measurement automation, data analysis, test and verification.
✅️ Commitment to facilitate effective cross-team knowledge transfer through clear documentation.

Apply here: https://t.co/Fq5Hqn0QQr

Please note: This Job is open to only those currently in the UK. Those outside the UK should not apply 

Please share this with others in the UK who may be Interested

 30-Second Stock Market Review
 🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥  

1️⃣ $META up 3% on a positive SemiAnalysis report stating that capex for 2027 is likely to remain intact, as the AI thesis remains solid. 
2️⃣ $BE up 8% amid ongoing volatility after the company recently announced a $25B framework from Brookfield to expand AI infrastructure. 
3️⃣ $AMD up 6% after Goldman Sachs raised its price target to $640. 
4️⃣ $IREN up 13% as AI compute and crypto stocks rallied. 
5️⃣ $TSLA up 6% amid recent production and delivery figures, along with momentum in robotics and physical AI stocks. 
6️⃣ $TSM up 4% on reports that the company is expected to raise its full-year revenue outlook to nearly 40% growth, up from 30%.
7️⃣ $CRDO up 10% as stocks in the optics and photonics sectors had relief rallies on Monday. 
8️⃣ $RKLB down 7% as space-themed stocks traded broadly lower. 
9️⃣ $DELL up 4% on positive comments from President Trump, urging consumers to buy the company’s products. 
🔟 $WULF up 4% on news that the company executed a 20-year lease deal with Anthropic at its data campus site in Kentucky. Also, the company signed an agreement to sell a 50.1% stake in the Abernathy joint venture to an investor group.

Market News

🆕 Bitcoin rose to nearly $64K on Monday after Trump made positive comments on crypto. 
🆕 Semiconductor stocks rallied after a nearly two-week slump. 

Upcoming Events 7/7/26

➡️ ADP Employment Change Weekly
➡️ 3-Year Note Auction
➡️ Earnings: $PENG, $EPAC, $KRUS, $SAR

text:
🚨 A new AI infrastructure bottleneck just got its own ETF — backed by SemiAnalysis

Introducing $LAZR — the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF, launched in direct partnership with @SemiAnalysis, the leading independent research shop in semis & AI infra.

$LAZR is pure-play exposure to the optics layer moving data between chips, servers & data centers at AI scale — the second critical bottleneck after memory (its sister fund $DISK)

Top 10 holdings:

Lumentum ( $LITE) 14.6% — lasers/transceivers powering high-speed data center links

Cash  holdings 11.4%

Aixtron 10.5% — German MOCVD deposition equipment for compound semis

$AXTI AXT Inc 9.7% — substrates underpinning optical & power devices

$AAOI Applied Optoelectronics 5.8%

Furukawa Electric 5.2%

$TSEM Tower Semiconductor 4.1%

Landmark Optoelectronics ~3.9%

$AEHR Aehr Test Systems 3.5%

Yangtze Optical Fibre & Cable 3.4%

Why it matters?? 
📊Goldman projects the AI data-movement/photonics market to grow 9x between 2026-2028. 
📈As bandwidth needs outrun copper’s physics, silicon photonics stops being a niche play and becomes core infrastructure.

$EUV VS $LAZR COMPARE CHART 👇

text:
Vergiss das 1. Halbjahr – ab jetzt wird neu gemischt! 📉📈

Statt auf den nächsten unprofitablen Hype zu hoffen, fokussiere ich mich auf diese 3 Kandidaten:
 $MSFT – bleibt das unzerstörbare Fundament der weltweiten Software-Infrastruktur.
$PLTR – beweist immer deutlicher, wie man mit KI-gestützter Daten-Analyse in der echten Wirtschaft massiv Geld verdient.
$SIVE – Sivers Semiconductors liefert die entscheidende Photonics-Lasertechnologie für KI-Rechenzentren. Die Volatilität nach der jüngsten Kapitalerhöhung bietet jetzt eine brutal spannende Einstiegschance.

Setzt ihr im 2. Halbjahr nur auf die bekannten Tech-Giganten oder habt ihr auch solche Small-Cap-Wetten im Depot?

text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha — July 3

The tape sold AI/semis like Meta capex = overcapacity. The best sources read the same data as proof of scarcity.

1. The neocloud selloff looks like a misread. SemiAnalysis flipped the bear narrative: Meta isn't cutting AI capex — it's turning compute into a business. 5GW+ already contracted in H1, and 2027 capex could come in above expectations. Short-term compute reportedly trades ~4x long-term neocloud rates — that's not what surplus looks like. Read-through: $NBIS $CRWV $IREN $CIFR. The outlier: $ORCL, flagged for weak compute monetization vs peers.

2. Memory upgraded from "shortage" to pricing power. The HBM chatter got specific: 2027 contract talk now includes 2–2.5x price increases vs broker estimates. CNBC: the Apple AI iPhone cycle could lift memory demand ~50%, with Apple hunting supply even from Chinese makers. Note — the same Apple-China headline traded as a bear signal yesterday; today's read is demand outrunning supply. Macro counterpoint: pricing near peak, trade crowded. $MU  $SNDK , Samsung and SK Hynix in Seoul.

3. Korea is a market-structure trade now, not just a memory proxy. Local channels near-unanimous: the selloff was technical — 2x leveraged products, foreign flows, pension rebalancing — not broken fundamentals. Earnings + HBM LTAs + cheap valuation on one side, index-shock sensitivity to every AI rumor on the other. $EWY.

4. Photonics/packaging — best undercovered signal of the day. From SemiAnalysis' ECTC notes: Marvell custom HBM cuts host ASIC area ~60%, TSMC direct-to-silicon cooling handles >5kW, optical interconnects becoming the core scaling path. Supply-chain angle: $LITE demand reportedly 30%+ ahead of supply, $COHR locking wafer supply via $AXTI, $CIEN as the cleaner transport play. Also $MRVL $IQE $TSM $INTC.

5. Physical AI: quieter, higher quality. BlueCrest disclosed a 5.6% stake in $CCXI  (the Agility Robotics proxy). $ONDS expanded its counter-drone TAM from federal to state/local. $BOT as the public basket. $OUST cooling off on fresh mentions.

The non-consensus read: if compute is still scarce and Meta is monetizing it, yesterday's selloff was positioning and liquidity — not a fundamental break.

text:
WHILE EVERYONE PANICS OVER CHIP STOCKS, THE REAL AI INFRASTRUCTURE PLAY IS QUIETLY SELLING OFF TOO.

$AMKR Amkor Technology down 12.93%. Semiconductor packaging and testing, critical link between chip design and the end product.

$LITE Lumentum down 9.09%. Optical components and photonics for datacenters and telecom, key infrastructure for AI networking.

$VRT Vertiv down 3.5%. Power and cooling infrastructure for data centers, the backbone that keeps AI compute running.

$MPWR Monolithic Power Systems down 3.27%. Power management chips used across AI hardware.

Different layers of the same AI buildout story, all getting hit together.

DYOR.

Posted by Antithesis Capital on LinkedIn in February 2026: 

Capitalizing on the Semiconductor Demand Cycle on the OTC market

SCI Engineered Materials (SCIA) is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. SCIA is mainly focused on markets within the photonics industry. Their main product is Sputtering Targets which are specialized solid materials used in thin-film deposition processes called Sputtering for semiconductors, displays, optics, and coatings. 

How Sputtering Works:
- A sputtering target acts as the source material
- The target is bombarded with high-energy ions 
- These ions knock atoms or molecules loose from the target's surface
- The freed atoms travel through the vacuum and deposit onto a nearby surface (the substrate), forming an extremely thin, uniform film 

Demand for sputtering targets continues to increase as the Semiconductor boom accelerates. Sputtering targets are  essential for depositing thin-film layers (interconnects like copper, barriers like tantalum/tungsten, insulators) during wafer fabrication via physical vapor deposition.

SCIA trades on the OTC market with a market-cap of $26M. Meanwhile, their balance sheet consists of the following: 

Cash: $9.5M
A/R: $900K
Inventory: $3M 
Marketable Securities: $3.3M
Total Liabilities: $6.3M 

$4M of the $6.3M in Total Liabilities is Customer Deposits which can be better defined as Order Backlog and signals on-going demand for SCIA’s products. SCIA had $1.9M and $2.2M in Net Income in 2024 and 2023 respectively. They’ve made $1.2M in Net Income through 9/30/2025. 

SCIA’s closest NYSE/NASDAQ comp is Materion Corporation (MTRN). Materion is an integrated producer of high-performance advanced engineered materials used in a variety of electrical, thermal, and structural applications. 

MRTN trades at an above market multiple with a PE of 163. In addition, they were recently upgraded to Overweight by KeyBank. According to the report, data points suggest MTRN is well positioned to capitalize on a global semiconductor consumable demand cycle which could feature higher aerospace and defense budgets, expanding clean energy businesses, and opportunities for margin expansion. 

We agree with KeyBank’s thought process and think SCI Engineered Materials will have a chance to capitalize on this same demand cycle. In our opinion, SCIA is a much more attractive way to play this demand cycle thesis due their rock solid balance sheet and reasonable PE multiple (16x). 

We are not the only ones who think this. Management, on 11/17/25, announced a $1 million share repurchase authorization. Clearly, they see the same value. 

SCIA is set to report FY25 earnings this week.

text:
The firm whose bearish CPO paper helped tank optical stocks just launched a photonics ETF and it's holding $SIVE.

Walk through what actually happened:

→ SemiAnalysis published a scathing piece on CPO delays and optical valuations. Optical names sold off 40-60%.

→ On June 30, SemiAnalysis co-launched the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF (NYSE: LAZR) — the first institutionally managed photonics ETF — in partnership with Tema ETFs.

→ As of July 1, that ETF holds 26,688 shares of $SIVE, bought around 48 SEK.

So the same research shop that helped drive the sector down is now buying into it through an institutional product — a 24-name concentrated fund built specifically for photonics and optical exposure.

I'm not going to call that anything it isn't. What I'll say is this:

When you get to publish the bearish note AND launch the fund that buys the dip, that's the most enviable seat in the market. And the name they put in the fund — out of every photonics company on Earth, in a concentrated 24-holding portfolio — was $SIVE.

An actively managed, SemiAnalysis-backed, institutional photonics fund looked at the entire optical universe and decided Sivers belonged in the 24.

That's not retail hype. That's a research-driven institutional desk putting $SIVE in the same basket as Lumentum, Coherent, and AXT.

The company keeps ending up exactly where the smartest money in the sector is looking.

$SIVE $LAZR

text:
Here's the takeaway that actually matters: $AAOI, $LITE, $COHR, $CIEN, $NOK are down 25-55% on sector weakness.

The photonics demand curve extends into 2027-2028 regardless of when CPO hits mass production. As clusters scale, copper hits a wall. Optical isn't optional. I'm buying the fear, not the narrative.

How far off their highs, and what you make just reclaiming them:

• $SIVEF $5.24 | down 53% | +115% to reclaim $11.25
• $AAOI $121 | down 48% | +93% to reclaim $234
• $CIEN $422 | down 34% | +51% to reclaim $638
• $LITE $725 | down 33% | +50% to reclaim $1,086
• $NOK $12 | down 31% | +45% to reclaim $17.45 (52w)
• $COHR $333 | down 24% | +32% to reclaim $440

And that's just to tag old highs.

text:
AXT เซ็นดีล 3 ปีกับ Coherent ขยายกำลังผลิตวัสดุชิปโฟโตนิกส์

AXT $AXTI เปิดเผยผ่านเอกสารยื่นต่อ SEC ว่าบริษัทในเครือ AXT-Tongmei ได้ทำข้อตกลงระยะเวลา 3 ปีร่วมกับ Coherent $COHR

ดีลนี้เกี่ยวข้องกับการพัฒนาและจัดส่ง substrate อินเดียมฟอสไฟด์ขนาด 6 นิ้ว ซึ่งเป็นวัสดุสำคัญในอุตสาหกรรม photonics หรือเทคโนโลยีการส่งข้อมูลด้วยแสง

ขยายกำลังผลิตในจีนเพื่อรองรับคำสั่งซื้อ
ภายใต้ข้อตกลงนี้ AXT จะขยายกำลังการผลิตที่โรงงานในกรุงปักกิ่ง ระหว่างปี 2026 ถึง 2028
โดยผลผลิตทั้งหมดจะถูกจัดสรรให้กับ Coherent ตามสัญญาที่กำหนดไว้

Coherent วางเงินล่วงหน้ากว่า 22 ล้านดอลลาร์
Coherent จะจ่ายเงินล่วงหน้าประมาณ 22.29 ล้านดอลลาร์สหรัฐ
เงินจำนวนนี้จะถูกนำไปใช้เป็นเครดิตสำหรับการซื้อสินค้าล็อตในอนาคต

อย่างไรก็ตาม เอกสารระบุเงื่อนไขสำคัญว่า
หาก Coherent ใช้คำสั่งซื้อตามขั้นต่ำไม่ครบ เงินล่วงหน้าอาจ “ไม่สามารถขอคืนได้”
แต่หาก Coherent เลือกยกเลิกดีลก่อนโดยไม่ผิดเงื่อนไข จะสามารถขอคืนเงินได้

ในทางกลับกัน
ถ้า AXT ส่งมอบกำลังผลิตไม่ได้ตามที่ตกลงนานเกิน 6 เดือน
Coherent มีสิทธิ์ยกเลิกสัญญาและขอคืนเงินที่ยังไม่ได้ใช้

text:
🚀  $SIVEF  🚀 

The ABC retracement in Wave C got extended but I believe we are close to a reversal.
I’m very bullish on this Ai core play who’s a provider of laser and packaged optics.

Daily chart 👇
Stopped on my line !
RSI low but not oversold, because the underlying trend strength is so strong. 
We already reach the max fib retracement 61.8%.
4H chart 👇
Bottom signal didn’t confirm and no bullish divergence on the RSI yet.
I will wait for a reversal sign to add.

NEWS
- Dilution announced on July 1st
- The company is actively preparing for a U.S. Nasdaq listing which could be a big catalyst for the next wave.

After looking at the chart closer I changed my wave count :
I now think that this correction is large cycle II with large wave III coming soon with targets $14-$16.50
Extended $19.50-$21.50

text:
かつて協働研究を行なっていた文化人類学者の宮永國子氏は、ワークショップに一時参加していた古河電工のベテランエンジニアの方を、「彼は天才です。」と言っていたのを思い出した。 🌹Grok『古河電工（古河電気工業）の基幹的イノベーションテクノロジーは、創業以来一貫して「メタル（Metals）」「ポリマー（Polymers）」「フォトニクス（Photonics）」「高周波（High Frequency）」の4つのコア技術を軸としています。これらは電線製造技術から自然に派生・進化したもので、現在も同社の全事業の基盤となっています。
4つのコア技術の概要
メタル: 銅精錬・金属加工技術から発展。導電材、超電導線材、カーボンナノチューブ、高機能銅箔・条など。
ポリマー: 絶縁材・機能樹脂の開発技術。自動車部品、放熱材、半導体製造用テープ、機能性フィルムなど。
フォトニクス: 光ファイバー・光デバイス・レーザー技術。情報通信の基幹を担い、近年は加工・医療分野にも拡大。
高周波: RF・高周波電子技術。無線通信部品、自動車電子部品、高速基板材料など。
これらを単独で深化させるだけでなく、融合させて社会課題解決型の新事業を創出している点が現在の特徴です。
過去の基幹的イノベーション（歴史的マイルストーン）
古河電工のイノベーションは、電線・電力インフラから情報通信・先進材料へという大きなシフトが特徴です。
1884年創業〜戦前: 足尾銅山由来の銅精錬から電線製造へ。日本初の海底ケーブル（1915年）や無装荷海底ケーブル（1937年、世界初）など、メタル＋ポリマーの組み合わせで社会インフラを支えました。
1974年: 世界初の光ファイバーケーブルフィールド試験に成功。これがフォトニクス事業の本格的な起点となりました。
1983年: 世界初の実用光ファイバ複合OFケーブルの商業化。
2001年: 米Lucent Technologiesの光ファイバ部門（OFS）買収。グローバル規模でフォトニクス技術・特許を強化。
2000年代〜2010年代: 超電導線材の開発が加速。CERN（欧州合同原子核研究機構）から特別賞受賞（2003年、LHC用）。2016年には希土類系超電導線材による永久電流技術を開発（MRIなど医療応用）。
2015年: 世界トップクラスの導電率を持つカーボンナノチューブの開発成功（メタル分野の先進材料イノベーション）。
この時期は「銅の会社から光の会社へ」の転換期であり、4コア技術の枠組みが明確に確立されました。
現在の基幹的イノベーションと応用
現在は4コア技術の深化＋融合＋デジタル化がキーワードです。古河電工グループVision 2030では、「情報・エネルギー・モビリティを組み合わせた次世代インフラソリューション」を目指しています。
1. フォトニクス分野の最先端
超多心・細径光ファイバケーブル: 2018年に世界最高水準のコア密度を実現した6912心細径超多心ケーブルを開発。以降も進化を続け、2026年3月には13824心（従来比約2倍容量）の量産を開始（AIデータセンター向け）。データ爆発（生成AI、5G/6G）に対応する基幹技術です。
光デバイス: 狭線幅・高出力マイクロITLA（2017年、100Gbps超光コヒーレント通信用）。
レーザー加工: Blue-IRハイブリッドレーザー「BRACE™」（2020年発売、日亜化学工業との協業）。青色レーザーと近赤外ファイバレーザーを組み合わせ、銅のスパッタレス高品質溶接を実現。EVバッテリーや電子部品製造に不可欠で、近年は5kW級青色レーザーモジュールまで進化。
2. メタル＋先進材料分野
超電導技術: 高温超電導集合導体の研究開発（NEDOプロジェクト）。浮体式洋上風力発電向け超高圧ダイナミック海底ケーブル（2019年受注）。核融合エネルギーや産業用大型電磁石への応用も進行中（2026年現在、英国Tokamak Energyとの協業も発表）。
軽量・高導電材料: カーボンナノチューブのワイヤ応用など。
3. ポリマー・高周波の応用
自動車電装システム（ワイヤハーネス、ステアリングロールコネクタ、先進安全センサー）。
放熱・冷却部品、半導体製造用テープ、高周波基板用銅箔など。EV・自動運転・5Gインフラを支えています。
4. 横断的イノベーション（特に重要）
DX（デジタルトランスフォーメーション）: 「デジタルトランスフォーメーション＆イノベーションセンター（DXIC）」を中核に、AI/IoTを製造現場に本格適用。SAP S/4HANA基幹システム刷新やデータ駆動型ものづくりを推進。
オープンイノベーション: 「Fun Lab®」をプラットフォームに、NEDOプロジェクト（金属積層造形へのフォトニクス活用など）やスタートアップ連携を積極化。
新分野展開: ライフサイエンス（光プローブやレーザー医療機器、蛍光シリカナノ粒子を用いた高感度検査キット）、インフラ維持管理、エアロスペースなどへ4コア技術を応用。』

text:
The AI Brain – 10 Infrastructure Layers related to neuroscience 
🧠 

1. Power & Electricity ⚡️ 
Neuroscience: Brainstem + Heart & Blood Vessels
AI infra: Power plants, electrical grids, substations
- This is the “blood” that keeps everything alive. AI needs huge amounts of electricity. No power = brain is dead

2. Cooling Systems 🧊 
Neuroscience: Body temperature control + Blood cooling
AI infra: Liquid cooling, big air conditioners, chillers
- AI chips get extremely hot, similar to running a marathon. Cooling stops the brain from overheating and breaking.

3. Datacenter Buildings 🏗️
Neuroscience: Skull + Outer Cerebral Cortex
AI infra: Huge warehouses full of server racks
- These are the “skull and brain tissue.” Big empty buildings that will later be filled. 

4. Compute Chips (GPUs & Accelerators) 💾
Neuroscience: Billions of Neurons (brain cells)
AI infra: The actual GPU chips and AI processors
- These are the individual “brain cells.” They do the heavy thinking and math for AI.

5. High-Speed Connections 🔌
Neuroscience: White Matter (nerve highways) + Corpus Callosum
AI infra: Fiber optic cables, networking switches, photonics
- Super-fast roads that let different parts of the brain talk to each other instantly.

6. Data Storage 💽
Anatomy: Hippocampus (memory center)
AI infra: Hard drives, SSDs, cloud storage systems
- This is where the brain keeps all its memories and knowledge safe for a long time.

7. Cloud & Neocloud Platforms 🧠☁️
Neuroscience: Neocortex (higher thinking areas)
AI infra: Companies like Nebius, CoreWeave, Crusoe, Lambda that rent out GPUs
- The “managers” that organize all the hardware and let people use the brain without owning it.

8. AI Software Frameworks 👨🏼‍💻
Neuroscience: Association areas (connecting ideas)
AI infra: Tools like PyTorch, Kubernetes, TensorFlow
- The easy-to-use “instruction manuals” that help people build and run AI on the hardware.

9. AI Models 🤓
Neuroscience: Prefrontal Cortex (planning & intelligence)
AI infra: Big models like GPT, Claude, Grok, Anthropic, etc.
- This is the actual “brain power” – the part that understands language, writes, codes, and reasons.

10. AI Agents & Robotics 🤖 
Neuroscience: Motor Cortex + Hands & Body (Peripheral Nervous System)
AI infra: AI agents, robots, self-driving cars, smart apps that take action
- This is the “body and hands.” It turns thinking into real-world actions.

Cooling Systems 🧊 Neuroscience: Body temperature control + Blood cooling AI infra: Liquid cooling, big air conditioners, chillers - AI chips get extremely hot, similar to running a marathon. Datacenter Buildings 🏗️ Neuroscience: Skull + Outer Cerebral Cortex AI infra: Huge warehouses full of server racks - These are the “skull and brain tissue.” Big empty buildings that will later be filled. 4. Compute Chips (GPUs & Accelerators) 💾 Neuroscience: Billions of Neurons (brain cells) AI infra: The actual GPU chips and AI processors - These are the individual “brain cells.” They do the heavy thinking and math for AI. 5. High-Speed Connections 🔌 Neuroscience: White Matter (nerve highways) + Corpus Callosum AI infra: Fiber optic cables, networking switches, photonics - Super-fast roads that let different parts of the brain talk to each other instantly. 6. Data Storage 💽 Anatomy: Hippocampus (memory center) AI infra: Hard drives, SSDs, cloud storage systems - This is where the brain keeps all its memories and knowledge safe for a long time. 7. Cloud & Neocloud Platforms 🧠☁️ Neuroscience: Neocortex (higher thinking areas) AI infra: Companies like Nebius, CoreWeave, Crusoe, Lambda that rent out GPUs - The “managers” that organize all the hardware and let people use the brain without owning it. 8. AI Software Frameworks 👨🏼‍💻 Neuroscience: Association areas (connecting ideas) AI infra: Tools like PyTorch, Kubernetes, TensorFlow - The easy-to-use “instruction manuals” that help people build and run AI on the hardware. 9. AI Models 🤓 Neuroscience: Prefrontal Cortex (planning & intelligence) AI infra: Big models like GPT, Claude, Grok, Anthropic, etc. - This is the actual “brain power” – the part that understands language, writes, codes, and reasons. 10. text:
$MRVL is becoming one of the most important names in the AI infrastructure buildout.

Nvidia’s Jensen Huang publicly called Marvell the “next trillion-dollar company” at Computex, citing its role in solving data movement across massive GPU clusters.

UBS just lifted its price target to $340 (from $230), spotlighting Marvell’s leadership in CXL the technology that lets processors and memory share resources far more efficiently inside next-gen AI racks.

Add in the industry’s first 102.4 Tbps switch purpose-built for AI/cloud, a strategic $NVDA  NVLink Fusion partnership with a $2B Nvidia investment, and Marvell’s optical/photonics push via recent acquisitions.

Despite the recent semis pullback, the fundamentals and strategic positioning look increasingly durable.

$MRVL is a High-conviction AI connectivity play for the multi-year buildout 🔌

text:
Now is not a time to be focusing on anything crypto related imo. We are in the midst of building a brain for the AI infrastructure buildout that will shape our future and the next 2 decades 

It’s a time to focus on efficiency, energy, and productivity, which cryptocurrency has proven to be inversely correlated to in recent years 

🧠 
Neoclouds = the Neocortex of the brain 
Datacenters = the cerebral cortex and cortical columns 
Power & electrical grids = brainstem and circulatory system
Cooling systems = thermoregulation & cerebral blood flow 
Fiber optics & photonics = white matter & corpus callosum
Data storage = hippocampus and long term memory 
Compute & robotics = cerebellum, PNS, and motor cortex
AI agents = prefrontal cortex and “consciousness”

{"from_index": 1261, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1272}, {"from_index": 1370, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1376}]}, "text": "Tech Parallels: Dotcom bubble & AI bubble Datacenters and neoclouds to AI infrastructure are what servers, networking gear, and early datacenters are to the dotcom/internet boom of the 1990-2000s - Computers became the essential “picks and shovels” that enabled everything that followed from e-commerce, search, social media, mobile apps, and eventually cloud computing Essentially, datacenters are the “new computers” that run AI chips or GPUs/accelerators Parallel Layers: 1. Infrastructure/Compute Layer (“new computers”): - Dotcom era: Servers + internet backbone/fiber. - AI era: AI-optimized datacenters + accelerators + neoclouds/hyperscalers - Key difference in scale/intensity: AI workloads are extraordinarily power-hungry. U.S. data center power demand is projected to roughly double from ~31 GW in 2025 to ~66 GW by 2027 - globally on track to double or more by 2030, with AI-driven accelerated servers growing ~30% annually This creates new bottlenecks in power generation, grids, and cooling (hence the shift to liquid cooling) 2. Platform/Distribution Layer: Dotcom era: Early ISPs, web hosts, and eventually cloud (AWS launched 2006) AI era: Neoclouds + hyperscalers renting GPU clusters, plus orchestration tools 3. Application Layer: Dotcom era: After infrastructure matured post-bubble → social media, mobile apps, SaaS, e-commerce explosion AI era: Ongoing. The datacenter/neocloud buildout is laying the rails for the application layer - real AI mania comes from execution of AI agents, vertical AI tools, robotics/embodied intelligence, scientific discovery acceleration, and personalized everything in the future"}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2066033922803114355"}}, "rest_id": "2068390362515988966", "source": "Twitter for iPad", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2402", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2073492205583335483", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1306", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The real story behind Sivers’ capital raise + H1 earnings delay 

Last week, Sivers completed a directed share issue of 700 M SEK (up from the initial 600 M target).
 The deal was heavily oversubscribed → they increased the size. Issued at 57 SEK (only 9.7% discount).

This is positive news: fresh capital to scale InP laser production, R&D, and capture AI / LiDAR opportunities.

BUT as always , bears are screaming dilution and bad governance. 

The facts now : 

The controversial part: H1 earnings has been delayed (originally expected early August → end of Augus → end of August) + waiving the lock-up from the April raise.

What happened:
April: Small raise with 180-day lock-up.
Late June: Big 700 M SEK raise → Pareto waives the lock-up for April participants.
H1 results moved after lock-up expiry.

Bear interpretation :  Management is panicked, delaying results so insiders can sell before releasing bad numbers.
Like this , if the earning sucks , they will be able to sell before 

Bull / realistic interpretation:
A 700 M SEK oversubscribed raise by institutions doesn’t fit the narrative of a sinking ship.
New investors did their due diligence. They wouldn’t have joined if fundamentals were rotten. 
Let’s be real : they have info retail don’t ! You think they would go in if it was the case ? 

By waiving the lock-up, management shows strong confidence in the future: they’re not afraid investors will dump, because those investors believe in the long-term story (CPO, partnerships, capacity scaling). 

The earnings delay likely allows them to come out with stronger guidance and better visibility on 2027 ramps (CPO, POET, GF SCALE, etc.).

In summary ,  This is a volatile micro-cap in scaling mode. There will always be FUD on dilution, timing, etc.
But when institutions pile in on a 700 M SEK raise and management waives lock-ups without fear, it carries more weight than conspiracy theories about earnings timing.

Long term bullish 
Still holding 

#SIVE #Sivers #Photonics #CPO #AI #Nasdaq #Semiconductors #Stocks

text:
Dear @TeamKeerthanaTN, @Keerthana4VNR, @Guidance_TN 
For Keerthana Office, CMO Tamil Nadu:

In the next 120 days, Tamil Nadu should run a “Central Fund Capture Mission” to bring the next wave of high-value investments in Deep Tech, Semiconductors, EVs, Drones, Defence, Electronics and Advanced Manufacturing. Please out compete Andhra Pradesh, they are going all out on a 24 by 7 campaign to bring all the industries to their state.

Should of Office of Keerthana also travel to Delhi for 3-4 days and talk to PMO office, and other Central Ministers?

Please check the speech of Lokesh Nara, they have a strong framework. Please hire BCG, Bain Consultants including experts from IIT-M, IIM-Tiruchirapalli, IIM-A if need be.

Top 15 Central Government funds/schemes TN can aggressively leverage:

🏗️ SASCI – Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment: Use 50-year interest-free Centre loans for industrial roads, power, water, logistics, plug-and-play parks and port-linked manufacturing zones.

🏭 BHAVYA Industrial Parks Scheme: Compete for new integrated industrial parks and position TN for sector-specific parks in EVs, electronics, defence, aerospace, drones and precision manufacturing.

🔬 India Semiconductor Mission / Semicon India: Build TN’s semiconductor pitch around fabs, ATMP/OSAT, compound semiconductors, display tech, chip design and supplier ecosystems.

⚙️ Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme: Target component makers for PCBs, camera modules, sensors, connectors, power electronics and mobile/electronics supply chains around Chennai–Sriperumbudur–Hosur–Coimbatore.

💻 Electronics / IT Hardware Manufacturing Incentives: Attract laptop, server, tablet, telecom device and data-centre hardware manufacturers using TN’s electronics talent and port advantage.

🚗 PLI Auto & Auto Components: Push TN as India’s EV + advanced automotive technology capital for e-2W, e-3W, e-4W, e-buses, ADAS, motors, controllers, powertrain and auto electronics.

🔋 PLI ACC Battery Storage: Bring cell manufacturing, battery packs, BMS, recycling, lithium/Na-ion innovation and grid-scale battery projects to Hosur, Chennai, Coimbatore and southern TN.

⚡ PM E-DRIVE Scheme: Use central support for EV adoption and public charging to make Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and industrial corridors EV-ready investment zones.

🚁 Drone PLI Scheme: Create a TN Drone Manufacturing & Testing Cluster for components, UAV assembly, agri-drones, defence drones, coastal surveillance drones and Drone-as-a-Service startups.

🛡️ iDEX + ADITI Defence Innovation: Connect TN Defence Industrial Corridor firms with startups, MSMEs, IIT-Madras, universities and DRDO-linked projects for cutting-edge defence technologies.

🎯 DRDO Technology Development Fund: Help TN companies pursue grants for indigenous defence systems, sensors, avionics, naval tech, aerospace components and dual-use deep-tech products.

🧠 IndiaAI Mission: Build TN AI compute, AI startup, data, skilling and applied AI programs for healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, governance, agriculture and Tamil-language AI.

🚀 ANRF / RDI ₹1 lakh crore Deep-Tech Fund: Position IIT-M, Anna University, research parks, startups and industries to capture long-tenor R&D capital for high-risk, high-impact innovation.

🌌 National Quantum Mission + Space VC Funds: Create TN quantum, photonics, sensing, satellite, propulsion and space-tech clusters linking Chennai, Coimbatore, Tirunelveli and Mahendragiri ecosystem.

🧵 PM MITRA + Startup India FoF / Seed Fund: Accelerate Virudhunagar textile park, technical textiles, manufacturing startups, deep-tech startups and MSME scale-up through Centre-backed startup and cluster capital.

Action for TN:

✅ Create a Central Scheme War Room
✅ Map each scheme to a TN district/cluster
✅ Prepare investor-ready project notes
✅ Add State incentives + land + power + approvals
✅ Pitch global investors with Centre + State package

Tamil Nadu can become India’s #1 destination for deep-tech manufacturing if every central scheme is converted into a targeted investment pipeline.

Please do also reach out to the Central Government and @svembu  to connect across India and abroad.

Please out compete Andhra Pradesh, they are going all out on a 24 by 7 campaign to bring all the industries to their state. Should of Office of Keerthana also travel to Delhi for 3-4 days and talk to PMO office, and other Central Ministers? Please check the speech of Lokesh Nara, they have a strong framework. Please hire BCG, Bain Consultants including experts from IIT-M, IIM-Tiruchirapalli, IIM-A if need be. Top 15 Central Government funds/schemes TN can aggressively leverage: 🏗️ SASCI – Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment: Use 50-year interest-free Centre loans for industrial roads, power, water, logistics, plug-and-play parks and port-linked manufacturing zones. 🏭 BHAVYA Industrial Parks Scheme: Compete for new integrated industrial parks and position TN for sector-specific parks in EVs, electronics, defence, aerospace, drones and precision manufacturing. 🔬 India Semiconductor Mission / Semicon India: Build TN’s semiconductor pitch around fabs, ATMP/OSAT, compound semiconductors, display tech, chip design and supplier ecosystems. ⚙️ Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme: Target component makers for PCBs, camera modules, sensors, connectors, power electronics and mobile/electronics supply chains around Chennai–Sriperumbudur–Hosur–Coimbatore. 💻 Electronics / IT Hardware Manufacturing Incentives: Attract laptop, server, tablet, telecom device and data-centre hardware manufacturers using TN’s electronics talent and port advantage. 🚗 PLI Auto & Auto Components: Push TN as India’s EV + advanced automotive technology capital for e-2W, e-3W, e-4W, e-buses, ADAS, motors, controllers, powertrain and auto electronics. 🔋 PLI ACC Battery Storage: Bring cell manufacturing, battery packs, BMS, recycling, lithium/Na-ion innovation and grid-scale battery projects to Hosur, Chennai, Coimbatore and southern TN. ⚡ PM E-DRIVE Scheme: Use central support for EV adoption and public charging to make Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and industrial corridors EV-ready investment zones. 🚁 Drone PLI Scheme: Create a TN Drone Manufacturing & Testing Cluster for components, UAV assembly, agri-drones, defence drones, coastal surveillance drones and Drone-as-a-Service startups. 🛡️ iDEX + ADITI Defence Innovation: Connect TN Defence Industrial Corridor firms with startups, MSMEs, IIT-Madras, universities and DRDO-linked projects for cutting-edge defence technologies. 🎯 DRDO Technology Development Fund: Help TN companies pursue grants for indigenous defence systems, sensors, avionics, naval tech, aerospace components and dual-use deep-tech products. 🧠 IndiaAI Mission: Build TN AI compute, AI startup, data, skilling and applied AI programs for healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, governance, agriculture and Tamil-language AI. 🚀 ANRF / RDI ₹1 lakh crore Deep-Tech Fund: Position IIT-M, Anna University, research parks, startups and industries to capture long-tenor R&D capital for high-risk, high-impact innovation. 🌌 National Quantum Mission + Space VC Funds: Create TN quantum, photonics, sensing, satellite, propulsion and space-tech clusters linking Chennai, Coimbatore, Tirunelveli and Mahendragiri ecosystem. 🧵 PM MITRA + Startup India FoF / Seed Fund: Accelerate Virudhunagar textile park, technical textiles, manufacturing startups, deep-tech startups and MSME scale-up through Centre-backed startup and cluster capital. Action for TN: ✅ Create a Central Scheme War Room ✅ Map each scheme to a TN district/cluster ✅ Prepare investor-ready project notes ✅ Add State incentives + land + power + approvals ✅ Pitch global investors with Centre + State package Tamil Nadu can become India’s #1 destination for deep-tech manufacturing if every central scheme is converted into a targeted investment pipeline. Lol… Non exclusive only matters when there is a second supplier.

Who exactly are they qualifying?

$GFS can qualify a second supplier whenever they want. But there is no known alternative in their qualification pipeline right now.

InP DFB lasers at this spec and volume is one of the hardest things to manufacture in photonics.

Non exclusive only matters if a credible replacement exists.
