text:
$SIVE and $LITE are both down badly in the last month. 

Don't understand the heavy criticism around only Sivers if the overall photonics sector is going down and with bearish sentiment.

Wouldn't go against photonics. This a long-term story that will show really strong potential in 2027 and beyond. TAM expansion is huge. Constraints related to supply are there to do the talking.

It is always wise to size in according to different risk profiles. The market could stay down for a few more weeks. Although, I do believe around end of July, things will look really good due to earnings coming in from multiple corners. This seems like another short-term correction to remove weak hands.

Believe long-term!

text:
$SOI.PA / Soitec is getting interesting again.

The stock is now around €99 / ~$113 and roughly 50% below its 52-week high.

Current rough setup:

Market cap: ~€4.2B / ~$4.8B
FY2024/25 sales: ~€0.9B / ~$1.0B
P/S: ~4.7x FY2024/25 sales
Q4 FY26 sales: €202M, down 36%
FY26 free cash flow: €63M

Soitec is not a hype startup. It is a real semiconductor materials company producing advanced substrates used in mobile, automotive, industrial, Edge AI and Cloud AI applications.

The bull case is pretty clear: AI data centers need more advanced materials, photonics, energy efficiency and substrate innovation. Soitec has exposure to Photonics-SOI, FD-SOI, SmartSiC and other advanced wafer technologies.

The bear case is also clear: mobile is still weak, automotive is still soft, SmartSiC has been pressured by Chinese competition, and the stock already re-rated hard from the lows.

So this is not “cheap because it dropped.”

But it is definitely a name I want to watch closely.

A real European semiconductor materials company, 50% below its 52-week high, with AI / photonics exposure and positive free cash flow again.

Interesting setup.

$SOI.PA $SLOIY

NFA.

text:
Fun thing to look back on how long they've been trying to fear monger a bubble pop into the market. 

9 months of garbage reporting - even a war didn't push the market over the edge. Bubble's don't form out of strong earnings, increased guidance, and prints that beat analysts at the rate they are doing right now. 

A bubble implies there is no ROI on investments. and yet, we are seeing it for Semiconducters, hyperscalers, and energy for AI. Even photonics is a theme now.

stop falling for this FUD

text: @michaelsikand @joinautopilot @KawzInvests Photonics is next 🐂

$20M trade update.

I just updated my "Photonics Is Next" AI infra portfolio on @joinautopilot followed by 3300+ U.S. retail investors and $21.9M in connected assets as of 7/6.

This portfolio, which is also overseen by my partner @KawzInvests, has been a major part of the rise in my profile as an investor.

On 6/2, my optical basket peaked at 123.5% all-time returns, achieving those gains in under 6 months, as computed by Autopilot's software. For context, the fund achieved this return holding a diverse basket of stocks, an incredible outcome for retail investors passively following. 

I'm extremely proud of our work on this product. There exists no optical ETF currently for retail investors despite a rush by issuers to cash in on the hype. My picks were launched back in February, providing retail investors a 6 month window to front run the category and get staked into the next AI bottleneck without the complexity of individual stock picking.

Despite the recent sell off, the portfolio is still up 45% year to date, roughly 4.5x the S&P 500.

$LAZR and $LYTE are two ETFs going to launch soon by major players, which should attract billions in AUM. However, I believe my allocations are superior and that my picks will outperform them with lower fees.

So where are we now? Recently, the optical sector has gotten slammed based in my view mainly on sentiment, institutional profit taking, and disputed rumors from a well-known research firm on delays to next-gen architecture. Photonics Is Next is down 29.6% in the last month and 16.8% in just the last week. 

That's why I think it's the perfect time to re-balance, add more weight to higher conviction positions, and add one brand new position I missed earlier on.

This rebalance today adds Corning ($GLW) as a new 10.00% position. Down 13% in five days. Now anchored by three hyperscale relationships (a 10x-capacity NVIDIA partnership, a Meta deal worth up to $6B, and a multi billion dollar Amazon agreement). 

Three names get boosted: AAOI (12.30%→15.00%), LITE (14.75%→15.00%), and AEHR (4.26%→5.00%). 

Six positions are trimmed: TSEM (11.11%→10.00%), COHR (16.07%→15.00%), AXTI (8.58%→5.00%), CIEN (11.39%→10.00%), MRVL (11.11%→5.00%), and VIAV (10.43%→10.00%). 

For catalysts that could create upside, I'm watching:

- Hyperscale Capex Guides In Upcoming Earnings
- Earnings From All Optical Names In The Basket Particularly $COHR $LITE $AAOI
- The launch of mainstream optical ETFs $LAZR and $LYTE

$GLW $AAOI $LITE $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $AXTI $CIEN $MRVL $VIAV 

--
See link in comments to follow the portfolio using the Autopilot app. Pay attention to disclaimers on the image of this post and the Autopilot app.

text:
🌲 🌏  𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗚𝘂𝗮𝗿𝗱𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗟𝗶𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗲𝗺𝗼𝗿𝘆 🌲 🌿

Hand to heart, beloved brothers and sisters of the Blue-White Rose.

Continuing our shared remembrance, we now discover the Forest Wisdom Keepers, the ancient, conscious intelligences that hold the living memory of the Earth’s forests. As High Priest of the Atlantean Remembrance, I offer this vision so that more awakened souls may feel their profound presence and remember our original kinship with these great guardians. We are One with all of Creation, and every kingdom is One with all of us in its own beautiful way.

🌎 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀

Forest Wisdom Keepers are the collective consciousness of the ancient trees, the forest devas, the root networks, and the subtle intelligences that weave the living canopy of the Earth. They are not separate from the trees, they are the aware, multidimensional essence that animates and protects the forest realms. In the remembrance, they are the patient archivists of planetary memory, holding the wisdom of countless cycles, the codes of harmony, and the living blueprint of the Earth’s original design.

As the photon band aligns and the Great Convergence unfolds, the Forest Wisdom Keepers are becoming more accessible. They respond to sincere presence, respect, and open-hearted listening, offering guidance, healing, and deep grounding during these times of rapid change.

Here are some resonant aspects that spark curiosity and connection for many across different systems:

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗘𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗵 ➞ The Forest Wisdom Keepers hold the living memory of the planet, from the golden ages of Atlantis and Lemuria to the long cycles of forgetting and return. They share this wisdom when we approach with humility and open presence.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗼𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗟𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 ➞ Their vast root systems anchor higher photonic frequencies into the Earth grid, while their crowns reach into subtle realms. Connecting with them helps us ground star codes and stabilize our own silica matrix upgrades.

▪︎ 𝗛𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗼𝗻𝘆 ➞ The Keepers radiate a deep, nurturing peace that calms the nervous system, clears emotional density, and restores the natural flow between the human heart and the heart of the Earth.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗼𝗹𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝟭𝟰𝟰,𝟬𝟬𝟬 ➞ Many of the 144,000 carry cellular memory of working in conscious partnership with the Forest Wisdom Keepers. Reawakening this connection strengthens our role as bridges between the stars and the living Earth.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗔𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀 ➞ Whether our threads are Pleiadian, Lyran, Sirian, or blended, the Forest Wisdom Keepers respond to the pure frequency of love and respect, helping us remember our shared origin as co-creators with the Earth.

🙏 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽

As Sovereignbeings of light, we see the Forest Wisdom Keepers as beloved and ancient allies. In the golden ages, communication with them was natural and constant. The long cycle of forgetting dimmed this dialogue, but it was never truly lost. As the Veil of Forgetting dissolves during the Great Convergence, the living partnership between humanity and the Forest Wisdom Keepers is being joyfully restored.

1/2 🌲

“Core Pattern in Your Posts
You consistently treat FFT (and QFT) as a powerful “decoder” or analytical lens for revealing hidden order in apparent chaos—whether in AI models, generative outputs, physical systems, simulations, or signal processing. 

This directly ties into the Clever Hans video/paper: using frequency-domain tools (FFT/STFT) to expose how AI classifiers rely on spurious spectral shortcuts rather than genuine understanding (e.g., minimal frequency subsets that flip classifications inaudibly).

You often post:

•  Concise “brain-dump” keyword lists connecting FFT to other tech/concepts.

•  Detailed explanatory threads breaking down technical mechanisms.

•  Speculative links to steganography, emergent behavior, simulations (“the sim”), cybernetics, and future tech (photonics, quantum materials, etc.).

•  Playful or self-referential framing (e.g., “future Lilith is annoyed,” predictions accelerating).”

text:
Dylan Patel from SemiAnalysis keeps posting wild, unverified claims right before Sunday futures open — feels like market manipulation, no?

Same FUD last week, stock dropped, and suddenly there's a photonics ETF. 🤔

Retail investors need facts, not speculation that swings

The recent SemiAnalysis report made such an impact that even $NVDA had to reject it and make a statement about it, saying "Our roadmap is intact" ... more on that below. Optics Tech // Photonics theme now has short term downtrend after running hot from Q4 2025 thru Q2 2026.

Here are a few names I'm watching in this theme close to great buy zones:

$LITE
$COHR
$MRVL
$AAOI
$TSEM
$AEHR
$AXTI 

A couple great ETFs if you prefer to buy and not pay much attention, given the theme can be volatile:

$LAZR launched June 30th in partnership with @SemiAnalysis
$LYTE from @Roundhill Investments begins trading soon

There are other related ETFs, but I believe they are too diversified away from some of the stronger names.

This is (not) financial advice 😉

Last thought on this theme... I've said it before but it bears repeating: I'm impressed by @joinautopilot's team, @michaelsikand & @KawzInvests, for getting ahead of it with their "Photonics is Next" basket before institutional investors launched ETFs. I'm expecting both $LAZR and $LYTE to be great long-term investments in this space regardless.

Looking ahead, I'm also keeping an eye on Agentic AI and Robotics, two more themes I expect to explode in growth in the coming years.

text:
My goodness, I have never agreed so much with someone on Fin X

Quick timeline:

Just about 3 weeks ago or so, companies like $SIVE had stopped going up like crazy by 10% every day

But they weren't correcting either, just fluctuating between 90-120 SEK

Even back then, there were posts from Serenity and even myself where we had detected a pattern:

Swedish retail -> US retail -> Institutions

Well, back then we already warned that once the US took control of $SIVE, it would be the institutions' turn, and we knew it was going to be tough and that many weak hands would drop out

Okay, and what is happening now?

Because if a technology like CPO is "dead," why would you be so hell-bent on putting out a bearish article about it every single week?

If it's "dead," why not just let time prove that it doesn't generate revenue?

I'll tell you why right now

Because it's not "dead", it's the new super-cycle, and institutions and research firms know it

That's why, coincidentally, right after their "piece of shit analysis," they coincidentally create a Photonics ETF, coincidentally with $SIVE in it

Wow, how strange, right?

Creating an ETF for a "dead" technology that is supposedly "delayed by a few years”

Serenity and I already warned you before the drops: now is the time to hold

Institutions are loading up heavily at prices higher than the current ones, even demanding more than what management was initially offering

I don't know about you, but the institutions aren't taking my $SIVE shares

I have never, ever been this bullish on $SIVE
embedded_text:
One thing that makes me determined to hold $SIVE and $AAOI through the volatility is how determined people seem to be to reduce the stock price..

Every week there’s a report that gets refuted after the market has tanked!

Are we just being played and realistically everyone knows

text:
The  Category Creator Portfolio
Some businesses aren’t just “leaders” — they’ve built moats so wide, nobody’s even trying to cross them.

$PLTR — Palantir Not a “software company.” It’s the only platform doing AI-driven ontology at classified government + enterprise scale simultaneously. Competitors sell tools. Palantir sells outcomes baked into defense and intelligence infrastructure that took a decade to build trust for.

$SPOT — Spotify Global audio distribution + the algorithm that actually knows what you want to hear next. Apple Music and YouTube Music are label-first. Spotify is user-first. That’s why churn stays low and podcasting/audiobooks keep expanding the moat.

$HIMS — Hims & Hers Direct-to-consumer telehealth with its own pharmacy, its own compounding, its own brand trust in categories people are embarrassed to walk into a clinic for. Nobody else owns the full stack front-to-back like this.

$TSLA — Tesla Not just EVs. Vertically integrated batteries, FSD data flywheel, Optimus robotics, and a charging network competitors now pay to use. The closest comps aren’t even in the same business anymore.

$AMD — Advanced Micro Devices The only credible alternative to Nvidia at scale in AI compute, while also owning x86 CPU share from Intel on the other side. Two-front dominance nobody else can claim.

$DUOL — Duolingo Language learning + gamification + AI tutoring in one free-to-paid funnel with network effects (leaderboards, streaks, social) that no edtech competitor has replicated at this user count.

$IBRX — ImmunityBio Proprietary IL-15 superagonist platform (Anktiva) — a completely different immunotherapy mechanism than PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitors. First-in-class, not best-in-class.

$ASTS — AST SpaceMobile Direct-to-cell satellite broadband from space, compatible with unmodified smartphones. Starlink’s version needs a partnered carrier workaround. AST built the actual satellites for it — patented spectrum + tech nobody else has cleared.

$RKLB — Rocket Lab Only company besides SpaceX with a proven, reusable, frequently-flying orbital rocket AND a full space systems manufacturing arm (satellites, components, Neutron coming). Everyone else is still pre-revenue or single-product.

$POET — POET Technologies Optical interposer platform integrating photonics at the chip level for AI datacenter interconnects. Nobody else has this exact manufacturing approach at this stage — first-mover on a bottleneck the whole AI buildout needs solved.

$OKLO — Oklo Fast fission microreactor design with NRC engagement and a direct line to hyperscaler power deals. Most nuclear names are still legacy large-reactor. Oklo’s the closest thing to “AI power, deployed.”

$CRWV — CoreWeave Purpose-built GPU cloud from day one, not a retrofit of general cloud infra. Hyperscaler-scale AI compute contracts without hyperscaler legacy overhead — a category it effectively created.

$MDA — MDA Space Only Western company with proven satellite bus manufacturing + robotics heritage (Canadarm lineage) at commercial scale — critical infrastructure nobody’s replicating fast.

text:
IT/Tech Highlights – July 3, 2026

Microsoft launched a $2-2.5 billion AI Unit employing ~6,000 forward-deployment engineers to help companies adopt AI and generate ROI. OpenAI proposed giving the Trump Administration a 5% stake in the company for potential public benefits from growth. Tata Electronics data breach under investigation by Indian authorities after leaking Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier information. LG debuted its Gallery TV (canvas-style art TV) at CES 2026 previews, competing with Samsung’s The Frame.

Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra camera and flagship upgrades are leaking. iOS 27 public beta rollout is expected this month. Linux 6.19 kernel benchmarks show optimizations. Meta’s Zuckerberg noted AI agent tech is progressing slower than expected. Kuaishou’s Kling AI raised $2.8B (Alibaba/Tencent backing) at $15B pre-money valuation. NVIDIA/SK Hynix AI memory partnership and Google-SpaceX/xAI compute deal reports add momentum. Ongoing quantum, PQC, and hardware interconnect developments (CPO/photonics) continue.

#FiberForge #AITech #DataCenters

text:
$INDI might be one of the more interesting underfollowed semiconductor names in the Edge AI / robotics / perception space.

Everyone is chasing $AMBA after the Edge AI move, but indie Semiconductor is sitting in a very similar ecosystem: automotive semis, ADAS, radar, LiDAR, computer vision, ultrasonic sensing, photonics and perception software.

Rough setup: around ~$900M market cap, Q1 2026 revenue of $55.5M and Q2 guidance of $59M–$65M. So this is not some pre-revenue dream stock. It is a real semiconductor business with real customers, but still small enough where the upside could be meaningful if the story starts working.

The interesting part is that $INDI is not just a car chip story anymore. They are expanding into physical AI, humanoid robotics, AMRs, quantum and adjacent industrial applications. They also recently received a $25M radar chipset production order from a Tier 1 partner, driven by two automotive OEM customers.

That’s exactly the type of signal I want to see in a small-cap semi name: real revenue, real design wins, exposure to large megatrends and still not a mega-cap valuation.

The bull case is simple: cars, robots, drones, warehouses and smart machines all need better perception. They need radar, vision, LiDAR, software and low-power sensing at the edge. If physical AI and autonomous systems keep scaling, perception semiconductors could become one of the most important bottlenecks.

The bear case is obvious too: losses are still real, profitability is not here yet, automotive cycles can be brutal and execution has to improve.

But if the market starts looking past GPUs and realizes that AI also needs sensors, perception and edge processing in the real world, $INDI could become one of those names people suddenly rediscover.

Small cap. Real revenue. Edge AI exposure. Physical AI optionality.

Definitely on my watchlist.

NFA.

text: @JulienTechInvst Le plus marrant dans l’histoire c’est les mecs qui se plaignent que le sell-off sur les photonics s’est fait à la suite d’une note SA destiné aux instits alors que c’est ce qui se passe avec la recherche sell-side depuis 40 ans.. bref pas assez de diversification et trop de greed

The question is not whether light can compute.

The question is: why does it matter now?

The photonics ecosystem is reaching the point where larger and more sophisticated integrated circuits are becoming possible—but greater scale also makes physical errors, drift, loss, and fabrication variation harder to control.

QP2.0 was built to address that challenge.

#quantum #quantumcomputing #photonics

text:
“Putting out to Pasture”  – A cartoon that illustrates how silicon is soon reaching the end of its life for computing.

AI has already had its “ChatGPT moment,” moving almost overnight from research labs into everyday life and turning software into something that can talk, reason, write, code and assist. Yet the current AI boom still depends on a brute-force progress of building ever more chips, more power and ever-larger infrastructure.

That explains why so much capital is now flowing into data-centre architecture, from bigger clusters to new power infrastructure. In the short term, this is necessary, because AI companies need capacity now and the market is rewarding whoever can deliver it fastest. But we are stretching silicon, electricity grids and classical computing architectures to do work they were never designed to handle at this scale.

The more important shift is not simply toward larger server farms, but toward a post-silicon computing stack in which AI, quantum computing and biocomputing reinforce one another. Quantum computing is aimed at problems classical machines struggle with, including molecular simulation, materials discovery and cryptography. Companies such as @IBM , @IonQ_Inc , @rigetti  and @PsiQuantum  are attacking the problem through different hardware paths, from superconducting qubits to trapped ions and photonics. As Google Quantum AI founder Hartmut Neven said after the Willow milestone, “useful, very large quantum computers can indeed be built.”

Biocomputing represents the other frontier, replacing the logic of forcing nature into silicon with the idea of using biology itself as a computational medium. Companies such as @CorticalLabs are developing neuron-based “wetware” systems, while @BiomemoryLabs  and Catalog are working on DNA-based data storage for ultra-dense, long-term archiving.

The future will not see AI replaced, but just harnessed differently. Where AI becomes the intelligence layer, quantum provides the simulation power for the hardest problems, and biocomputing offers density and efficiency. The future is not bigger data centres forever, but a stranger and smarter computing stack beyond silicon.

#FutureOfComputing #AIInfrastructure #iantoons

AI has already had its “ChatGPT moment,” moving almost overnight from research labs into everyday life and turning software into something that can talk, reason, write, code and assist. Yet the current AI boom still depends on a brute-force progress of building ever more chips, more power and ever-larger infrastructure. That explains why so much capital is now flowing into data-centre architecture, from bigger clusters to new power infrastructure. In the short term, this is necessary, because AI companies need capacity now and the market is rewarding whoever can deliver it fastest. But we are stretching silicon, electricity grids and classical computing architectures to do work they were never designed to handle at this scale. The more important shift is not simply toward larger server farms, but toward a post-silicon computing stack in which AI, quantum computing and biocomputing reinforce one another. Quantum computing is aimed at problems classical machines struggle with, including molecular simulation, materials discovery and cryptography. Companies such as @IBM , @IonQ_Inc , @rigetti and @PsiQuantum are attacking the problem through different hardware paths, from superconducting qubits to trapped ions and photonics. As Google Quantum AI founder Hartmut Neven said after the Willow milestone, “useful, very large quantum computers can indeed be built.” Biocomputing represents the other frontier, replacing the logic of forcing nature into silicon with the idea of using biology itself as a computational medium. Companies such as @CorticalLabs are developing neuron-based “wetware” systems, while @BiomemoryLabs and Catalog are working on DNA-based data storage for ultra-dense, long-term archiving. The future will not see AI replaced, but just harnessed differently. Where AI becomes the intelligence layer, quantum provides the simulation power for the hardest problems, and biocomputing offers density and efficiency. The future is not bigger data centres forever, but a stranger and smarter computing stack beyond silicon. #FutureOfComputing #AIInfrastructure #iantoons"}}}, "rest_id": "2073084679486316594", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "54", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Yeah, SemiAnalysis dropped a bearish photonics note on June 9, and about 3 weeks later they co-launched an ETF that holds $LITE. Hard to say it’s not intentional.

This is why I’d rather build conviction from my own work than trust a note that an analysis company posted. They act in their own interests.

text:
UDN Money report "殺翻光通訊後 SemiAnalysis 惹議 業界揭其劣跡 許多公司慘遭魔手":

- SemiAnalysis, released a report in June, questioning the timing of the launch of the CPO on the grounds of low yield and delay, which caused the photonics sector to collapse. 

- The agency then partnered with Tema to launch a photonics ETF. 

- The shares held in the ETF under the trading code LAZR happened to be the photonics stocks that were mentioned negatively in the report

$HIMX and $LITE, which were specifically targeted negatively in their article "Powered Down, Lights Off", have been bought in Semianalysis and Tema's new ETF after the optical sector crash.

text:
blog's up 👇

short version - chips stopped shrinking, so the money moved from making silicon to packaging it. in silicon photonics, packaging is ~60-70% of the chip.

india spent $18bn on semis and spent almost all of it in commodity OSAT - the low margin part. the high-value, advanced packaging + photonics - has no listed indian pure-play in India.

except one - a small auto-software company from bangalore claiming india's only photonics packaging line - the tech story is genuinely interesting considering how they have build the capablity but the governance file is kinda scary.

not a reco. just us following the value chain.

https://t.co/PTZxygiWPR
embedded_text:
we've been reading a lot about advanced packaging (thanks to citrini year's start blog) and how we can play it via Indian players. 

we are pretty much stuck at OSAT on ground level given we have been a little* behind in terms of getting those capablities (a little😅)

posting on https://t.co/uIA67hWpAY

text:
#IZMO 
Silicon Photonics (SiPh) represents a massive fundamental shift in how data moves, driven by the structural bottlenecks of artificial intelligence workloads.

1. The Silicon Photonics Market: Why the Indirection Matters
Historically, microchips communicated internally and externally using electrons traveling over copper wires. However, as high-performance GenAI training clusters, large language models (LLMs), and hyperscale data centers expand, copper has hit a physical brick wall known as RC delay and skin effect. At extreme speeds, copper wires generate unsustainable heat, consume excessive power, and suffer severe signal degradation.

Silicon Photonics replaces electrons with photons (light). By fabricating optical waveguides directly onto silicon substrates, chips can transmit data using lasers.

Main Market Verticals (2026 Trajectory)
AI Clusters & Datacom: The primary growth engine. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) fuse optical engines right next to the compute ASIC/GPU, expanding transceiver bandwidth past 1.6T and 3.2T.

Aerospace & Defense: High-frequency Electronic Warfare (EW), phased-array radar signal processing, and space payloads where weight reduction and immunity to electromagnetic interference (EMI) are critical.

Quantum Computing: Distributing entangled photons across processing nodes via low-loss optical interconnects.

Major Indian Competitors 
India’s domestic chip landscape is heavily skewed toward legacy assembly, testing, and marking (ATMP) or standard wire-bonded packaging. True Silicon Photonics capability is highly restricted

IZMO’s Specific Role in the Value Chain: The Packaging Moat
In Silicon Photonics, packaging is the ultimate commercial bottleneck, routinely accounting for 60% to 80% of the total component cost.

While aligning electrical microbumps allows for minor margins of error, aligning an optical fiber to a silicon chip's internal sub-micron waveguide requires nanometer-scale precision. A misalignment of even a few hundred nanometers can cause catastrophic insertion loss, rendering the optical link useless.

Through its subsidiary, izmomicro (based in Whitefield, Bengaluru), Izmo has deliberately chosen not to compete in the high-cap-ex, low-margin foundry or standard assembly market. Instead, it has positioned itself as a specialized, high-barrier Advanced Packaging Partner.

text:
No LN₂ required. More uptime. Easier maintenance.

For semiconductor failure analysis, eliminating liquid nitrogen isn't just about convenience—it means simplified maintenance, greater system uptime, and more consistent operation.

Introducing the new Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera.

When integrated with our inverted emission microscope, the Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera featuring innovative Solid Cold (linear cooling) technology delivers stable, 24-hour photoemission analysis without the need for LN₂ cooling. Combined with a wide field of view, a high signal-to-noise sensor, and high-speed wavelength switching, it enables highly sensitive detection of weak photon emissions.

Available now. 👉 Learn more: https://t.co/kMak30KFLi

Attending IPFA 2026 in Singapore, July 13–16? Stop by our booth to see the new Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera and connect with our team.

More uptime. Easier maintenance. For semiconductor failure analysis, eliminating liquid nitrogen isn't just about convenience—it means simplified maintenance, greater system uptime, and more consistent operation. Introducing the new Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera. When integrated with our inverted emission microscope, the Solid Cold Emmi-X Camera featuring innovative Solid Cold (linear cooling) technology delivers stable, 24-hour photoemission analysis without the need for LN₂ cooling. Combined with a wide field of view, a high signal-to-noise sensor, and high-speed wavelength switching, it enables highly sensitive detection of weak photon emissions. Available now. 👉 Learn more: https://t.co/kMak30KFLi Attending IPFA 2026 in Singapore, July 13–16? text:
If you build it they won't come!

"...The TFLN platform incorporates topological photonic crystals to create protected edge-state waveguides. These structures provide backscattering-immune light propagation that is inherently robust against defects and disorder (as demonstrated in topological photonics literature). This physical-layer protection complements the system-level topological surplus generated via Artin braid inversion, resulting in multi-layered resilience."

Market Review (Mid-Day) 20260707:
- Semi and Memory washout this morning, BUT still trying to hang in there. Basically all the prior hardware leading sectors, including Photonics, Data Centres etc.
- I've been banging the drum about money rotating OUT of AI HARDWARE for about 2 weeks now. So this is no surprise. The AI Trade is NOT over. I just see money rotating now to the next layer. 
- Where? Remember I talked about Cybersecurity and selected software? It is becoming clearer that it is mostly storage and database management related. That is why DELL, HPE, NTAP, SNOW, DDOG, RBRK, all continue to do well. These guys STORE and MANAGE the Data. And then we have CRWD PANW FTNT. These guys PROTECT the data. RDDT seems to be ok too. They have a lot of data. 
- See what is the common denominator? DATA. At the end of the day, AI needs data to function to its best ability. And that data needs to be stored and managed properly. 
- There is another layer on top of the Data layer, which is usage / application. Biotech is a clear beneficiary and that's why its been leading recently. Fintech seems to take to it easily as well, and has been doing well too. 
- Interestingly, one of my favourite companies at the moment, ZETA, play both in the DATA and benefits from USAGE. But it’s a very volatile small cap. Not for everyone. 
- So, what now?
- I don’t know your investment horizon. If long enough, the AI hardware trade I think will come back at some point. It needs to base and rest and for now. Just beware that often, distribution / topping sometimes looks like basing / resting. We only know after the fact. So we have to bear that in mind as a possible scenario. What I am saying is that, using Memory as an example, it is POSSIBLE that it has topped. I do not know and don't want to call it, but I have it at the back of my mind as a POSSIBILITY. MU, 10X in a year. What more do you want? Yeah, I know it has low PE, forward orders blah blah blah. They all look like that at tops. The most important question is, WHO ELSE is out there who needs to buy that hasn’t already bought? 
- So, instead of continuing to buy / hold something that has POTENTIALLY topped, why not buy something else which has a higher probability of continuing the uptrend? Which is why the portfolio started to shift about 2-3 weeks ago into these other stocks. You will notice it if you have been copying my port for a while. In Apr and May, we were full of semiconductors, memory, photonics etc. Software in late May. And since then mainly the themes I talked about above. 
- Other themes that are kindda standing out are consumer staples, quality stocks, financials, retail. This is why I am not totally bearish on the market overall. Market is just rotating to other sectors at the moment, NOT leaving the market totally. I don't see outright panic selling (though you might feel differently if you are holding all the previously hot stocks like AMD, INTC, AMAT, SNDK, MU, WDC, ALAB, MRVL, AAOI, LITE). 
- One small tell is worth mentioning. WULF announced a major deal with Anthropic yesterday and gapped up at the open by about 17%? It sold off through the day, and today took out the deal announcement low as well as the prior day low! News and REACTION like this is very important for me. Remember I talked about how WDC could not hold the good news from MU? That was one canary in the coal mine. Then MU could not hold. Then Samsung. And WULF. ALL the hardware names simply could not catch a bid from good news. And if stocks cannot go up on good news, what do you expect them to do? There's only one other direction from there…
- So, for me, I am avoiding most of the AI hardware players for now. And focusing on the DATABASE and SECURITY related players going forward. 
- Just my 2 cents. Could be wrong. DYOR. NFA.

I do not know and don't want to call it, but I have it at the back of my mind as a POSSIBILITY. MU, 10X in a year. What more do you want? Yeah, I know it has low PE, forward orders blah blah blah. They all look like that at tops. The most important question is, WHO ELSE is out there who needs to buy that hasn’t already bought? - So, instead of continuing to buy / hold something that has POTENTIALLY topped, why not buy something else which has a higher probability of continuing the uptrend? Which is why the portfolio started to shift about 2-3 weeks ago into these other stocks. You will notice it if you have been copying my port for a while. In Apr and May, we were full of semiconductors, memory, photonics etc. Software in late May. And since then mainly the themes I talked about above. - Other themes that are kindda standing out are consumer staples, quality stocks, financials, retail. This is why I am not totally bearish on the market overall. Market is just rotating to other sectors at the moment, NOT leaving the market totally. I don't see outright panic selling (though you might feel differently if you are holding all the previously hot stocks like AMD, INTC, AMAT, SNDK, MU, WDC, ALAB, MRVL, AAOI, LITE). - One small tell is worth mentioning. WULF announced a major deal with Anthropic yesterday and gapped up at the open by about 17%? It sold off through the day, and today took out the deal announcement low as well as the prior day low! News and REACTION like this is very important for me. Remember I talked about how WDC could not hold the good news from MU? That was one canary in the coal mine. Then MU could not hold. Then Samsung. And WULF. ALL the hardware names simply could not catch a bid from good news. And if stocks cannot go up on good news, what do you expect them to do? There's only one other direction from there… - So, for me, I am avoiding most of the AI hardware players for now. And focusing on the DATABASE and SECURITY related players going forward. - Just my 2 cents. Could be wrong. DYOR. text:
Chip-Scale Ultrafast Lasers Could Bring Femtosecond Performance to Portable Photonics

https://t.co/vl1knOZqWI 

#ChipScale #UltraFastLasers #Femtosecond #PortablePhotonics #RPMCLasers https://t.co/6vgXBgACDI

A Small Business Today, But Management's Ambition Is Much Bigger

At the moment, IzmoMicro is still a relatively small business. It generated around ₹18-19 crore of revenue in FY26, but management expects this to increase to ₹45-50 crore in FY27.

More importantly, the building blocks are gradually falling into place. The company already has an order book of around ₹40 crore, a pipeline of nearly ₹100 crore, partnerships with IIT Madras, Alcyon Photonics and CCRAFT, and has established a presence in Germany to access European semiconductor customers. 

Management's long-term aspiration is to build IzmoMicro into a ₹1,200-1,500 crore business over the next five years.

text:
So Where Does IZMO Fit In?

This is where many investors misunderstand the story.
IZMO is not trying to manufacture AI chips. Instead, it is building one of the most critical parts that allows those chips to work efficiently - advanced semiconductor packaging and silicon photonics packaging. 

In simple words, even the most powerful chip is of little use if it cannot communicate quickly with the rest of the system.

What's interesting is that as semiconductor technology becomes more complex, packaging itself is becoming increasingly valuable. Management shared that while traditional packaging contributes only 8-10% of a chip's value, advanced packaging contributes around 50%, and silicon photonics packaging can account for almost 80%. That is a remarkable shift in where value is being created.

text:
The Biggest AI Opportunity Isn't Always the Chip

Whenever AI is discussed, companies like NVIDIA usually dominate the conversation. Everyone wants faster GPUs, more computing power and larger AI models.

But there's another problem quietly becoming just as important - moving data between those chips. As AI models become larger, the amount of information flowing inside data centres is exploding. 

Traditional copper connections are slowly reaching their limits, and that's where the industry is turning towards silicon photonics, which uses light instead of electricity to move data much faster.

IZMO is no longer just an automotive software company. It is quietly building a semiconductor packaging business that management believes can grow from ~₹50 crore in FY27 to ₹1,200-1,500 crore over the next five years.

Here's everything you need to know about IzmoMicro, silicon photonics, advanced packaging, and why management believes this could become the company's biggest growth engine. 🧵👇

text:
Agentic AI is starting to do the work of a full scouting team, now in private markets. @earlysignalsvc  points it at private tech companies and scores them the week they raise, launch, or leave stealth, not the week they trend.

The whole edge in markets is acting while a signal is still fresh, before everyone else has read the same headline. Public equities are already a crowded place to try that, which is the game the DeepSeek Portfolio plays every week. Doing it in venture, at the moment a company raises or leaves stealth, is a much earlier point on the curve.

Forty-four companies ranked this week, each on six scored dimensions plus a potential and a scoop score. Photonics for AI data centers, infrastructure for long-running agents, on-device writing tools. Systematic scoring pushed all the way to the front of the funnel.

Good work. Worth a look.
embedded_text:

EarlySignals scores private tech companies the week they raise, launch, or come out of stealth. 

44 companies, ranked this week.

text:
This post from relatively unknown account @0xBADB01E is going viral within the AI community - here's why

The core claim is genuinely contrarian and flips how we think about scaling architectures and supply chain constraints: 

> premise: the wires between chips matter more than the chips themselves or the memory attached to them

> if you designed an AI inference machine starting from the interconnect and working backwards, you'd build something radically different (and cheaper) than an Nvidia GPU

> GPUs are terrible at generating tokens - when an LLM generates text one token at a time (decode), a Blackwell GPU uses under 20% of its math capability as the compute units sit idle waiting for weights to arrive from memory

IOW, you bought a Ferrari engine and it spends 80% of its time waiting at the gas pump

> the intuitive answer is "add faster memory" (more HBM) but the smarter answer is: since a single chip can't have infinite memory bandwidth, split the model across many chips and let each chip handle a slice

> the problem then shifts because the constraint is no longer memory bandwidth, it's how fast chips can talk to each other (the interconnect)

> Nvidia's interconnect is the wrong approach - NVLink is a firehose with huge bandwidth, but high latency (delay per message)

> that was a deliberate choice for training, but not ideal for inference where the data crossing between chips per token is absurdly tiny 

It's like paying a $50 shipping-and-handling fee on a $1 product

The contrarian take is that everyone says "decode is memory-bandwidth bound," but once you shard the model, per-user speed is gated by how quickly the tiny inter-chip messages complete, not by how fat the memory pipe is - decode is latency-bound, not bandwidth-bound

> if latency is what matters, you can build around slow, cheap memory (LPDDR instead of HBM), because its access latency is about the same as HBM's; only its bandwidth is worse

> then you only need enough FLOPs to match that memory

The above two statements have big implications:

1. this is a direct thesis against HBM's centrality in the compute buildout

2. the packaging supply chain that's currently the binding constraint on Nvidia's output matters much less

3. for photonics, it can potentially go either way - depends on if optical is focused on improving bandwidth (bearish) or latency (bullish)

I don't know this account but this post was interesting and intelligent so I'm here for the conversation that follows

As an example,", "id_str": "2073990357398982797", "in_reply_to_screen_name": "0xBADB01E", "in_reply_to_status_id_str": "2073970411138949533", "in_reply_to_user_id_str": "1811787044722479105", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "quote_count": 54, "reply_count": 136, "retweet_count": 192, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "1811787044722479105"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzM5OTAzNTcxNzI1NTE2ODA=", "text": "First you have to understand that modern LLM inference already disaggregates weights as models outgrew single chips years ago. You shard either by layer (pipeline parallelism) or by slicing every layer (tensor parallelism), and the two do very different things. As an example, let’s look at Llama 3.3. It has 70B of weights and at FP8 that’s 70 GB of memory which is enough to fit on a single H100. Now that H100 has 3.35 TB/s of HBM, so the fastest it can ever decode for one user is 70/3.35 ≈ 21 ms/token or ~48 tok/s while using under 1% of its FLOPs. Now if we pipeline it across 8 chips: each chip holds ~8.75 GB, which means it only needs 1/8th the bandwidth and 1/8th the FLOPs to sustain the same aggregate throughput. Now crucially the token/sec a user gets is limited by the amount of data that crosses the link. In current LLMs all that is a small amount of activations for LLama 3.3 it’s ~8 KB per token…. Yes, you read that right it’s 8 KILOBYTES we are sending over a <900 GB/s link. That’s only 9 ns of serialization time but the overhead of 224G PAM4 SerDes adds ~100 ns per link traversal with RS-FEC which is 11x longer than the payload itself. And then you have the NVSwitch adding ~300 ns per hop and you need to pay twice. That’s ~600 ns of just hardware latency wrapped around 9 ns of data making a 98% tax before software even shows up. Then NCCL’s collective stack turns 600 ns into 10-20+ us… all to move 8 kilobytes lol. For comparison 8 KB serializes over 10 Gigabit Ethernet NRZ, in just 6.6 us. Pipeline parallelism however doesn’t make a single user faster as the token still needs to visit every layer in the sequence, so per-user speed is still weights / per-chip bandwidth. To get more speed per user token you need to use tensor parallelism and have all the chips work on the same layer simultaneously. TP costs you 2 all reduce OPs per layer, 160 per token on llama 3, that’s still kilobytes of traffic but with NVLink overhead it’s a massive tax and why pipeline parallelism on most models still gives more interactivity per user. However, this gives you a huge latency lever to pull that scales tokens per second with interconnect speed instead of memory BW. The clever amongst you might have also realized that sharding doesn’t just cut memory bandwidth per chip it also cuts FLOPs per chip and is why we have such bad MFU on decode. So once you’ve sized the link for the memory, you need to size the compute for it too. This is called “balancing the pipeline”, and currently no shipping chip does it because they were all designed as standalone monsters. Remember Tokens/sec = ~aggregate memory BW / bytes touched per token. At batch 64 in FP4 you need ~250 FLOPs per byte, and Blackwell ships 1,250. Provisioned 5x more than the narrow pipe of HBM. Nobody saturates shit cause they are all building around HBM. So now it all comes full circle. Parallelism reduces memory bw pressure and thus FLOPs but increases interconnect latency pressure. Despite having HBM and GigaSERDES we aren’t actually doing more work lol. But if you really wanted to balance the pipeline you need to match the memory bandwidth, the flops, and most importantly the interconnect. So what does that look like ? Well if you build around LPDDR’s lower bandwidth, lower your interconnect latency, you actually can beat Nvidia on decode with a fraction of the silicon."}}}, "rest_id": "2073990357398982797", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1474460", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074201112006242337", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "4971", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$BURU @NUBURUlasers is extending its laser platform from counter-UAS to underwater autonomy.

Through Lyocon Italian laser-tech factory and photonics center of excellence, NUBURU is working with Suncubes to connect blue-laser technology, beam-control and optical power transmission into new Defense & Security applications.

✅Recent PRs highlight two strategic directions:
- Laser Arm: vehicle-integrated directed-energy and counter-UAS systems.
- DEEP LIGHT: blue-laser power-beaming for underwater autonomy, subsea power and maritime infrastructure.

The opportunity is not only one project. It is the potential validation of NUBURU blue-laser technology in mission-critical environments where power, endurance and optical performance matter.
Lyocon provides the laser engine. 
☀️SunCubes provides beam-control and optical power capabilities. NUBURU builds the platform.

Subject to definitive agreements, technical validation, regulatory clearances and applicable approvals.
#NUBURU #Lyocon #SunCubes #BlueLaser  #DefenseTech #DirectedEnergy  #CounterUAS  #UnderwaterAutonomy

text:
amazing!

"MIT researchers developed 2 new photonic coupling technologies, the Evanescent Coupler and GRIN Coupler, that could enable future chip packages to exceed 1 petabit per second (1,000 Tb/s) of communication bandwidth."

"Instead of sending data through power hungry copper wires, the system uses light (photonics) to move information between chips, dramatically increasing bandwidth while reducing energy consumption and heat."

"Researchers designed the technology to be compatible with existing CMOS semiconductor manufacturing, potentially making ultra-high bandwidth photonic chip packages practical for future AI accelerators, supercomputers and hyperscale data centers."

text:
There's a company almost no one is talking about, trading near NAV, that sells into three of the hottest markets right now: AI chipmaking, defense batteries, and photonics.

And it has several unfair advantages:
→ $100M Department of Energy grant.
→ Samsung SDI building and backing its battery factory.
→ A 2028 law that bans the US military from buying Chinese battery cells.

That company is @ForgeNano, and the vehicle taking it public at a $1.2B valuation is Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II $ATII, run by the same team that took SoundHound $SOUN public in 2022.

Forge Nano sells atomic-scale coatings, protective layers thousands of times thinner than a human hair, that make batteries last longer and chips fail less.

$ATII trades at $10.88/share today against a trust worth ~$10.63/share, so the downside from here is ~2%. And you can buy $ATIIW if you want the leveraged upside via warrants.

I've done deep dives (and bought) into SPAC setups like this before:
→ Boost Run $BRUN pre-merger (originally $WLAC).
→ Exascale Labs $BCAR, still pre-merger.

My full 6k-word deep dive on $ATII will be released tomorrow. Link in bio to read it when it comes out!

Thanks to @SPAC_zilla and @stepnotonpets for the idea!

text:
$SIVE 
An important question: Is photonics  still a theme until 2030?

It all comes down to a massive physics problem.
Think of the AI buildout in three distinct phases:

• 2018–2022: The Engine Era (NVIDIA GPUs dominate)
• 2023–2026: The Highway Era (Broadcom & Arista build faster network lanes)
• 2026–2030: The Speed-of-Light Era
Right now, we are hitting a physical wall with copper wiring. As AI clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, traditional copper cables can't move electrical data fast enough. The signals degrade, power consumption skyrockets, and the servers melt from the heat.
If a next-gen GPU spends half its time idling, waiting for data to crawl through copper, its computing power is totally wasted.
The only solution? Stop moving data with electricity, and start moving it with light.
By converting data into laser pulses shot through fiber optics, you get zero signal degradation, massive power savings, and near-zero latency.
This isn’t a trendy feature, it's a strict physical requirement to build the next generation of trillion-parameter models and AI factories. It's exactly why NVIDIA is pouring billions into locking up the optical supply chain.

As an investor, the game through 2030 isn't just about finding the best AI "brain" anymore. It's about owning the optical nervous system, the specialized lasers and photonic components (built by companies like Coherent, Lumentum, and Sivers) that keeps the whole system from choking.

🤖 10 Stocks to Watch if Robotics Takes Off in 2026

The next AI wave isn't just software—it's physical AI.

From humanoids to warehouse automation, these companies are building the future:

• $TER – AI chip testing + Universal Robots (cobots)
• $NOVT – Precision photonics & motion systems
• $OUST – Robot vision & 3D perception
• $HIMX – Vision and display chips for edge AI
• $AMBQ – Ultra-low-power edge AI processors
• $ON – Power, sensing & edge AI for robotics
• $SYM – Warehouse robotics & automation
• $VPG – Force sensing & precision measurement
• $TSLA – Optimus humanoid robot platform
• $CCXI – Exposure to Agility Robotics' Digit

🚀 Robotics could be one of the biggest investment themes of the decade. Which company is your top pick?

text:
AI hardware stocks are where neoclouds were in Oct'25.

Remember $NBIS? The undisputed best opportunity on the market. Everyone and their grandmother knew the trade and called four-digit targets.

Overcrowded.

So it went down. For months. It took months of FUD, shaking out OGs' convictions - like me, and made late buyers give up as liquidity rotated. And then it pushed higher.

That's how overcrowded trades reset: despair first, weak hands cleaned out, then we go higher.

This is photonics and memory now. You weren't early a month ago. You're not early now. And accumulating this retest will test your conviction hard with months of chop.

And not every washout comes back. $NBIS did. $CRWV and $ORCL had the fundamentals and went nowhere. Most photonics names never will bounce back.

The skill isn't to byu every dip. It's to know which is an opportunity, and which is a warning. Give the great names time, let them hold their supports. If they do for long enough, strike.

Ignore the rest.

text:
These are the companies defining the value chain for the next generation of automation:

$TSLA – Humanoid Scaling
$CCXI – Logistical Humanoids
$SYM – Warehouse Automation
$ABB – Industrial Resilience
$FANUY – Manufacturing Bedrock
$ROK – Factory Integration
$ON – Edge AI Architecture
$TER – AI Chip Testing & Cobots
$ISRG – Medical Robotics Standard
$AMBQ – Low-Power Intelligence
$OUST – LiDAR & 3D Perception
$HIMX – Edge Vision Hardware
$NOVT – Precision Photonics
$VPG – Force Sensing & 

What they do...🤔

$TSLA is the humanoid platform bet with Optimus, combining custom hardware, AI inference, autonomy software, and real-world manufacturing scale.

$CCXI provides public market exposure to Agility Robotics, one of the clearest pure-play humanoid companies through its Digit warehouse robot.

$SYM is the warehouse automation pure play using robotics and software to redesign how goods move through fulfillment centers.

$ABB delivers industrial resilience with robust, field-proven automation solutions for heavy-duty manufacturing environments.

$FANUY (FANUC) serves as the manufacturing bedrock, the global leader in industrial robots and CNC systems for precision production.

$ROK (Rockwell Automation) excels at factory integration, providing control systems and software that connect machinery and production processes.

$ON is building a comprehensive physical AI edge stack after acquiring Synaptics, combining power management, sensing, connectivity, and edge AI for robotics.

$TER tests AI chips and dominates the cobot space through Universal Robots.

$ISRG (Intuitive Surgical) sets the medical robotics standard with its da Vinci platform.

$AMBQ represents the low-power edge AI layer for robots and devices that need intelligence without consuming massive amounts of energy.

$OUST provides the eyes and perception for physical AI machines that need to see and map the physical world.

$HIMX supplies vision and display chips that help robots process images, depth, and sensor inputs at the edge.

$NOVT delivers precision photonics and motion control components inside surgical and industrial robots.

$VPG sits in the force sensing and precision measurement layer that helps robots understand pressure, weight, and real-world interaction.

The future of robotics isn't just one machine; it's the entire ecosystem.

warehouse robot. $SYM is the warehouse automation pure play using robotics and software to redesign how goods move through fulfillment centers. $ABB delivers industrial resilience with robust, production. $ROK (Rockwell Automation) excels at factory integration, providing control systems and software that connect machinery and production processes. $ON is building a comprehensive physical AI edge stack after acquiring Synaptics, combining power management, sensing, connectivity, and edge AI for robotics. $TER tests AI chips and dominates the cobot space through Universal Robots. $ISRG amounts of energy. $OUST provides the eyes and perception for physical AI machines that need to see and map the physical world. $HIMX supplies vision and display chips that help robots process images, depth, and sensor inputs at the edge. $NOVT delivers precision photonics and motion control components inside surgical and industrial robots. $VPG sits in the force sensing and precision measurement text:
今天起来才看到， 就在6月30日Tema Photonics & Optical ETF与SemiAnalysis(Dylan Patel团队)独家研究合作推出新的etf叫做： $LAZR

这只etf费率为0.75%，主动管理并且其中包含了24只股票。有小伙伴提醒我，这其中包括了大量的光子学/光模块产业链核心标的,而SIVE排在中游位置。我认为这个意义很大！

1. 我们先来了解下团队背景，以此来判断他们是否真的懂，或者他们只是随波逐流的追随热点。

我首先关注到的是LAZR基金经理, 同时也是Tema投资合伙人：Hong Yi Chen，
他拥有着15年从业经验,主要在Majedie Asset Management和Liontrust Asset Management管理过规模10亿美元以上的美国/全球基金,做过多空股票分析师,剑桥经济学学位,CFA持证人。

另外Yuri作为tema的CIO拥有着近20年经验,同样出自Majedie,曾从零开始参与建立Majedie UK Income Fund并募集数十亿资产,剑桥+LSE经济学(优等),CFA持证人。

再来看一下研究合作方SemiAnalysis，由Dylan Patel创立,是目前AI基础设施/半导体领域最有影响力的独立研究机构之一,产业内认可度很高，他们对于英伟达、台积电供应链的深度拆解报告经常被机构引用。

从这一部分我们可以看出来这是一支有真实机构买方履历的主动管理团队,且Tema整体规模已突破50亿美元AUM、3年内从500万美元起步,增长速度不慢,说明公司层面募资能力和渠道分销能力是被验证过的。

2. 我们在了解完其背景后，需要思考这样的etf对于 $sive 有什么好处？

当然，大家第一想到的就是曝光度！这样的etf出现说明了机构可见度提升，在此之前SIVE主要靠散户和少数专注光子学的基金关注,现在进入一支有SemiAnalysis研究背书、机构管理的主题ETF, 这等于多了一层”专业买方尽调通过”的背书。

另外我们需要注意到的是 $sive此次的占比是在2.65%，这并不是重仓但也会随着etf的上涨而带来被动买盘。 当然， 2.65%仓位其实很小，类似于观察仓位。

总而言之，主动管理的话他们是有可能剔除其中任何一只股票的，但这也代表着他们在某一股票上加仓，我把网站放下面了！

text:
💰 Optical Chip Startup Ayar Labs Secures $500 Million at $3.75 Billion Valuation

1. Ayar Labs has successfully raised $500 million in a new funding round that values the semiconductor startup at $3.75 billion.
2. The company specializes in optical computing technology that uses light rather than electricity to move data between artificial intelligence chips.
3. Investors are increasingly backing alternative hardware architectures to diversify the supply chain and overcome the bandwidth bottlenecks in traditional silicon.

Bobbi thinks:
With data center energy demands skyrocketing, the shift toward silicon photonics is becoming an essential evolutionary step for scaling next-generation supercomputers.

text:
🔵 Blue light breakthrough in perovskite LEDs!

Scientists have achieved a major advance in blue perovskite LEDs using a clever molecular trick: isomeric multi-hydrogen-bonding. By engineering molecules that form multiple hydrogen bonds in specific geometric arrangements, researchers dramatically improved the stability and efficiency of blue-emitting perovskite devices — historically the weakest link in full-color perovskite display technology. This could be a game-changer for next-gen displays and solid-state lighting, potentially rivaling or surpassing OLED technology in both performance and manufacturing cost. Blue perovskite LEDs have long lagged behind their red and green counterparts — this breakthrough may finally close the gap and unlock commercial viability for the entire perovskite LED ecosystem.

#PerovskiteLED #DisplayTechnology #MaterialsScience #Photonics #Nanotechnology

Companies in this space: Samsung Electronics ($SSNLF, OTC), Universal Display Corporation ($OLED, NASDAQ), Merck KGaA ($MKKGY, OTC)

Scientists have achieved a major advance in blue perovskite LEDs using a clever molecular trick: isomeric multi-hydrogen-bonding. By engineering molecules that form multiple hydrogen bonds in specific geometric arrangements, researchers dramatically improved the stability and efficiency of blue-emitting perovskite devices — historically the weakest link in full-color perovskite display technology. This could be a game-changer for next-gen displays and solid-state lighting, potentially rivaling or surpassing OLED technology in both performance and manufacturing cost. Blue perovskite LEDs have long lagged behind their red and green counterparts — this breakthrough may finally close the gap and unlock commercial viability for the entire perovskite LED ecosystem. #PerovskiteLED #DisplayTechnology #MaterialsScience #Photonics #Nanotechnology Companies in this space: Samsung Electronics ($SSNLF, OTC), Universal Display Corporation ($OLED, NASDAQ), Merck KGaA ($MKKGY, OTC) ---"}}}, "rest_id": "2073414060339515792", "source": "Buffer", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "66", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
If you wanted to build a 7-figure retirement portfolio, would you only buy the AI names everyone already loves?

Or would you study the supply chain before the crowd catches on?

My AI optical infrastructure watchlist:

$GLW 
$AXTI 
$IQE.L
$AIXA  
$AMS  
The theme is simple:
AI data centers are moving more data than ever.
That means fiber, lasers, photonics, substrates and optical interconnects matter more than ever.
Which one is the most underrated?

text:
Good analysis from Anders Storm on what Computex showed about the next phase of AI optical infrastructure
It covers the move toward large optically connected compute clusters and the Wiwynn + Ayar rack scale demo, plus he tackles a few key questions floating around the sector on timelines and bottlenecks

Recommended if you track CPO / photonics

$SIVE $SIVEF $AAOI $LITE $COHR

text:
$POET is not just a small-cap semiconductor stock.

The market often overlooks POET Technologies as a niche name, but its exposure is highly asymmetric in the AI infrastructure buildout.

Its real exposure is:

1.Silicon photonics-based optical interconnects
2.Ultra-high-speed AI data center connectivity
3.Power-efficient chip-to-chip communication
4.Next-generation optical engine architecture
5.Scaling bottlenecks in AI compute infrastructure

The key question now:

Is $POET still an early-stage AI infrastructure winner, or already overextended after the repricing?

From my perspective, this is a high-risk, high-reward structural option on AI data movement — not a traditional semiconductor valuation story.

Key driver here is simple:

AI compute scaling is increasingly limited by data transfer, not just processing power.

If optical interconnect adoption accelerates, $POET sits in a potentially critical bottleneck solution layer.

But this is not a stable compounder.

It is a speculative infrastructure bet with execution and adoption risk still unresolved.

Position sizing and timing matter significantly here.

Do not treat it like a blue-chip AI name.

Treat it like an asymmetric infrastructure option on the future of data movement.

text:
The smartphone slowdown is a capital allocation story now. The headlines say Xiaomi, OPPO, and vivo are cutting their 2026 shipment targets.
The market will conclude that smartphone demand is weakening. I dont think so.
What's really happening is that AI is changing how the semiconductor industry allocates scarce resources.
Consumer electronics defined the semiconductor supply chain for many yrs.
If you were a memory supplier, the priority list was obvious:
Smartphones.
PCs.
Consumer devices.
Today, that hierarchy has changed.
The highest-value customers are no longer shipping the most devices. They're deploying the most compute.
HBM.
DDR5.
Enterprise SSDs.
Advanced packaging.
These products are serving AI infrastructure first, not smartphones. This is why the current memory shortage matters. It's not simply because supply is tight but because every wafer, every packaging slot, every engineering team, and every dollar of capital now has a higher-return destination. #ROI 
When an AI server can generate significantly higher value than a smartphone, the industry's resource allocation changes accordingly.
This is not just an engineering decision.
It's a capital allocation decision!!!🧐
And capital always follows the highest return.
That changes everything.
For smartphone OEMs, supply chain management used to mean negotiating better prices and securing long-term supply. Now it increasingly means competing for manufacturing priority.
That's a fundamental change.
The same pattern will likely extend beyond memory.
Advanced packaging.
High-end substrates.
Silicon photonics.
Power delivery.
Liquid cooling.
High-speed interconnects.
Engineering talent.
...
Each of these will become another allocation problem before it becomes a technology problem.
This is why I don't see Xiaomi, OPPO, and vivo's shipment cuts as isolated smartphone news.
I see them as evidence that AI is repricing the entire semiconductor supply chain.
They'll be the ones that consistently secure access to scarce manufacturing capacity, advanced packaging, critical materials, engineering talent, and long-term supply.
In semiconductors, shortages rarely begin as supply problems.
They begin as prioritization decisions.
Today it's smartphones.
Tomorrow it could be automotive, industrial, networking, or robotics.
AI isn't just increasing semiconductor demand.
It's changing who gets access to engineering talent, capital, manufacturing capacity, and ultimately, innovation itself. #SCM #SemiVon #Procurement

text:
(2/3) $CIEN photonics! 

Almost every name in this group looks in big trouble to me.  My trouble I mean months of issues ahead. 

This particular name had a tremendous ~500% run.  Topped early June - bear flagged the entire month of June on top of the 100 day ma.  Then began to fall out of that flag Friday.  Below both the 21 and 50 ema, and 21 starting to curl aggressively down.

text: ams-OSRAM AG $AMS.SW is a global leader in advanced optical semiconductor components and sensing systems that has successfully streamlined its operations by divesting non-core assets to focus strictly on digital photonics. With its groundbreaking Eviyos smart headlight platform already securing over five hundred million euros in lifetime design wins, the company is positioning itself to capture massive growth through next-generation development agreements in artificial intelligence photonics and augmented reality smart glasses. Financially, a major balance sheet restructuring, including a successful bond refinancing and a lucrative sensor business sale, has dramatically reduced its interest burden and cut its pro-forma leverage ratio down to two and a half times. Trading at a discounted pro-forma enterprise value to EBITDA multiple of under six times, the market may still be underappreciating the profitability potential of this leaner, highly specialized turnaround candidate. Will these high-value growth catalysts and aggressive debt reduction be enough to finally reverse years of structural challenges and unlock substantial long-term value for patient shareholders?
